# Dunerds: No Data Beyond Our Reach - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

Dunerds:NoDataBeyondOurReach
============================

Hildobby (Head of Data at Dragonfly) and Danning Sui (Data Scientist at Flashbots) explore the limitless potential of on-chain data and its applications in this #DuneCon2024 session.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 3, 2024•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

In this insightful **#DuneCon2024** session, **Hildobby**, Head of Data at Dragonfly, and **Danning Sui**, Data Scientist at Flashbots, reveal the immense potential of on-chain data and how Dune is revolutionizing blockchain analysis. They showcase their groundbreaking work in areas like Ethereum staking, NFT wash trading, and the rising adoption of private mempools, all while highlighting the untapped opportunities in blockchain data science.

This talk dives deep into the mechanics of data curation, heuristics, and tagging, demonstrating how meticulous research powers the dashboards we rely on. Hildobby and Danning explore how advanced tools like Spellbook tables and custom community-uploaded datasets extend Dune's capabilities far beyond what meets the eye, even allowing insights into off-chain activity.

The speakers also discuss the evolution of decentralized finance, highlighting the growing complexity of DEX routing, private mempool adoption, and the pivotal role of major players like Coinbase. They share candid advice for data scientists looking to enter the field, emphasizing the endless opportunities for innovation across blockchains, bridges, and wallet analytics.

Stay tuned to the end for engaging audience Q&A, where topics like data poisoning, community incentives, and curating reliable datasets are debated. Whether you're a blockchain enthusiast, data scientist, or industry professional, this session offers an unparalleled look at the cutting edge of on-chain analytics.

Fredrik and Mats mostly share stories about their journey and give glimpses into the future of Dune. # Beyond the 10-K: Revolutionizing Financial Transparency with On-Chain Data - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

Beyondthe10-K:RevolutionizingFinancialTransparencywithOn-ChainData
==================================================================

Sonya Kim, Chef at Steakhouse Financial, explores how on-chain data is transforming financial transparency and redefining traditional reporting methods at #DuneCon2024
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 3, 2024•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

At **DuneCon 2024**, **Sonya Kim** from **Steakhouse Financial** shared how on-chain data is revolutionizing financial reporting in DeFi, bridging traditional finance (TradFi) rigor with blockchain transparency. She highlighted the growing importance of fundamentals in driving token value, showcasing how protocols with improving per-token metrics significantly outperform those without.

Drawing inspiration from the SEC-mandated 10-K reports, Steakhouse Financial created a blockchain-native _2023 MakerDAO Retrospective Report_. This 50-page document offers real-time insights into MakerDAO’s financial operations, such as its evolution from crypto-backed collateral to integrating real-world assets like U.S. Treasury Bills. This pivot enabled MakerDAO to achieve record revenues in 2023, with 95% of collateral deployed into earning opportunities, outperforming even traditional banks in efficiency.

Sonya emphasized how on-chain data democratizes access to financial insights, enabling anyone to analyze operations in real time. She concluded by highlighting DeFi’s potential to surpass traditional systems in transparency, scalability, and trust, driven by tools like Dune Analytics. "On-chain transparency isn’t just better reporting—it’s a paradigm shift," she stated.

# Behind The Blocks - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

BehindTheBlocks
===============

A panel discussion with blockchain leaders Keone Hon (Monad), Smokey the Bera (Berachain), Stani Kulechov (Avara, Aave, Lens), and Camila Russo (The Defiant).
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 3, 2024•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

At **DuneCon 2024**, an exclusive panel discussion brought together blockchain leaders Keone Hon (Monad), Smokey the Bera (Berachain), Stani Kulechov (Avara, Aave, Lens), and Camila Russo (The Defiant). This insightful session explored the core elements of building successful Web3 projects, addressing decentralization, finding product-market fit, community building, tokenomics, technology, and regulation.

Key topics discussed included:

*   Navigating the decentralization spectrum and balancing trade-offs for user experience.
*   Lessons learned from achieving product-market fit through perseverance and iteration.
*   Strategies for building resilient communities without relying on airdrops.
*   The importance of innovative technology in Web3 success.
*   The evolving landscape of crypto regulation and the best global hubs for blockchain development.

This panel offered invaluable insights from some of the industry's top visionaries, providing lessons and strategies that are shaping the future of Web3.

👉 Don’t forget to subscribe for more DuneCon content and expert blockchain insights.

# Blockspace Abundance: What Do We Do With It? - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

BlockspaceAbundance:WhatDoWeDoWithIt?
=====================================

0xRob, Resident Wizard at Dune, explores the opportunities and challenges presented by blockspace abundance in this thought-provoking #DuneCon2024 session.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 3, 2024•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

At **#DuneCon2024**, **0xRob**, Resident Wizard at Dune, explored the rapid expansion of blockchain capacity and its impact on innovation.

#### Highlights

Rob examined the explosion of blockspace, driven by Ethereum's evolution and the rise of Layer 2 solutions like Optimism, Base, and Arbitrum. While capacity has increased, Rob stressed the importance of using it for **complex and meaningful on-chain activity**, not just more transactions.

Key insights:

*   **Complexity on Layer 2s**: Metrics such as gas usage and function diversity show more sophisticated transactions happening on Layer 2 chains, with Base and Arbitrum leading.
*   **Real Innovation or Noise?**: Rob challenged the audience to consider whether this complexity serves real-world applications or just advanced trading and MEV strategies.

Rob concluded that Layer 2s are driving innovation but called for deeper analysis to ensure the growing capacity fosters meaningful progress.

# Changes to Dashboard Refreshes - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

ChangestoDashboardRefreshes
===========================

We're changing how we refresh dashboards to deliver better user experience and ensure the sustainability of our platform. Here's what you need to know.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

News•July 13, 2023•2 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

![Changes to Dashboard Refreshes](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F40a7af59a509cb7ea152ac389bbd40459909bc36-2400x1350.png&w=3840&q=75)

Greetings, wizards! We have some important updates for you about how we keep dashboards data up-to-date.

Historically, we’ve refreshed dashboards whenever their data is older than 6 hours on Dune and 24 hours on Spark. This feature, known as Time To Live (TTL), has served us well.

In Our commitment to making data as accessible and useful as possible, we’vetaken on the expense of refreshing a vast array of dashboards – some of which, unfortunately, do not generate broad value for our community.

Our vibrant community of wizards has put a lot of time and effort into creating these dashboards, which serve as public goods **We believe it is our responsibility to keep these public goods useful;** The associated costs we’ve incurred to maintain the hundreds of thousands of dashboards on Dune, we’ve happily borne as an act of goodwill towards our dedicated Dune community.However, indiscriminately refreshing all dashboards, regardless of their community value, is not sustainable.

Over the past months, we've implemented changes to enhance the transparency of data freshness of Dune dashboards, and provided wizards with more tools to manage their data freshness to best suit their needs. This has included introducing a clearer indication of a dashboard's data age, adding a Run button to dashboards, and recently launching Dashboard Scheduling. Furthermore, we've initiated charging credits when users apply parameters to a dashboard, to prevent subsidizing dashboard runs that don't offer broad value to our community.

Next week, on Monday, July 24th, we will be turning off auto-refresh (TTL) for all content on Dune. **We will no longer indiscriminately refresh all dashboards.**

While we’re turning off TTL, we will simultaneously roll out a new functionality to dynamically refresh our most popular dashboards. **When you visit dune.com, our most accessed content will still be up-to-date.**

The frequency of refreshing will vary, with more popular dashboards refreshed more often, while less popular ones will be updated less frequently. As a result, highly popular dashboards will often be refreshed more regularly than they are now, allowing us to deliver an improved user experience while keeping the costs of maintaining up-to-date public goods dashboards at a reasonable level.

We appreciate your understanding as we make these changes to ensure the sustainability of our platform and the quality of the data you depend on. As always, we're committed to delivering the best possible experience to our Dune community.

Let the data flow!

‍

# Chat with Lisan al Gaib, Vitalik Buterin, at DuneCon2024 - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

ChatwithLisanalGaib,VitalikButerin,atDuneCon2024
================================================

Our mystery guest, Vitalik Buterin, joins Mats Olsen (CTO & Co-Founder of Dune) for a captivating discussion at #DuneCon2024.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 3, 2024•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

In this highly anticipated session from **#DuneCon2024**, Ethereum co-founder **Vitalik Buterin** joins **Mats Olsen**, CTO and Co-Founder of Dune, for an in-depth and candid conversation. Together, they explore Ethereum’s design principles, blockchain transparency, the role of Layer 2s, and the future of privacy and scalability on the blockchain.

From the implications of Ethereum’s transparency to the challenges of privacy protocols, Vitalik reflects on the intentional trade-offs made during Ethereum’s inception. He shares insights on Ethereum's evolution, explaining how advancements in zk-proofs and privacy layers are shaping new possibilities. The discussion also dives into the multi-layered blockchain ecosystem, with Vitalik commenting on the potential for Layer 3s, proof aggregation, and application-specific chains.

For fans of blockchain innovation, this talk is packed with Vitalik's thoughts on Ethereum’s biggest opportunities ahead, including decentralized social networks, financial applications, and blockchain-based credible commitments. The conversation is peppered with personal anecdotes, technical deep dives, and a forward-looking vision for how Ethereum and blockchain technology can redefine trust, openness, and collaboration globally.

Stay until the end to hear Vitalik’s candid Q&A with the audience, where he tackles tough questions on Ethereum’s governance, redesigning blockchain consensus, and even what he’d create if starting a new blockchain from scratch. This session is a must-watch for anyone passionate about blockchain technology and its future.

# Creating and Managing Dashboards - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

CreatingandManagingDashboards
=============================

Master Dune dashboards with this step-by-step guide—learn how to create, customize, and manage data-rich dashboards to showcase blockchain insights and streamline your analytics workflow.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•August 16, 2024•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

The video provides a comprehensive guide on how to create, edit, and manage dashboards on the Dune platform. Key points include:

1.  **Creating Dashboards**: Users can create dashboards by selecting the "Create" button, where they can name, customize the URL, and choose privacy settings.
2.  **Dashboard Features**: Dashboards can contain various visualizations like charts, counters, time series, and tables. Users can interact with these visualizations, such as running all queries or specific ones within the dashboard.
3.  **Customization**: Users can add visualizations and text widgets using markdown, which supports images, bullet points, and links. Dashboards can also be organized into folders, and queries within them can be scheduled to refresh automatically at specified intervals.
4.  **Additional Functions**: The platform allows users to take screenshots of dashboards, fork their own dashboards (not others), link to a GitHub repository for collaboration, and add or remove stars as a rating system.
5.  **Managing Dashboards**: Users can change the privacy settings, archive dashboards (which hides them but allows for unarchiving later), and delete them if necessary.
6.  **Further Learning**: For more detailed information, users are encouraged to visit the dashboards section of the Dune documentation.

Overall, the video aims to help users effectively create and manage dashboards on Dune, leveraging its various tools and settings.

# Cracking Solana Staking: Native, Pools, and LSTs Unlocked - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

CrackingSolanaStaking:Native,Pools,andLSTsUnlocked
==================================================

Andrew Hong dives into Solana staking, exploring native staking, staking pools, and liquid staking tokens in this insightful #DuneCon2024 session.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 3, 2024•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

In this highly technical **#DuneCon2024** session, **Andrew Hong**, ex-Headmaster at Dune, delves into the intricate mechanics of Solana staking. Covering native staking, staking pools, and liquid staking tokens (LSTs), Andrew unpacks the complexities of Solana’s delegated proof-of-stake system and its implications for decentralization, user incentives, and DeFi applications.

The presentation begins with a foundational explanation of Solana's staking architecture, including the role of epochs, validator dynamics, and reward mechanisms. From there, Andrew explores the nuances of managing stake accounts, their lifecycle, and the challenges of tracking Solana’s unique staking behaviors due to the low-level operations that bypass typical transfer logs.

Andrew also provides an in-depth analysis of LSTs on Solana, comparing them to Ethereum’s approach while highlighting Solana’s experimentation-friendly ecosystem. With tools like the S Pool Factory enabling rapid LST creation, Andrew explains how different LSTs distribute stakes across validators, their scoring mechanisms, and the rise of single-validator LSTs.

Key takeaways include:

*   The dominance of key players like Helus and Coinbase in Solana’s staking ecosystem and the ongoing debates around decentralization and commission rates.
*   How LSTs drive innovation and DeFi adoption on Solana while remaining underutilized compared to Ethereum (6% vs. 40% of total stake).
*   Challenges in data tracking and decoding Solana-specific staking actions for analysis.

The session concludes with Andrew addressing audience questions on validator dynamics, LST adoption, and the future of Solana’s staking ecosystem. Packed with actionable insights and technical depth, this talk is essential viewing for anyone interested in the evolving staking landscape of Solana.

# Creating Visualizations (Charts) - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

CreatingVisualizations(Charts)
==============================

Learn how to build and customize powerful charts on Dune—this guide walks you through creating data visualizations, formatting results, and enhancing your dashboards for clearer blockchain insights.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•August 16, 2024•2 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

The video provides a guide on how to create and customize visualizations on the Dune platform after writing a query. Key points include:

1.  **Overview of Visualizations**: The video introduces different visualization types, including charts, tables, and more, explaining how each can be formatted, including axes, legends, and colors.
2.  **Formatting and Customization**: Users learn how to format raw query results, such as renaming columns, adding dollar signs, rounding values, and adjusting number formats for readability. The video also covers how to customize visualization elements like colors, legends, and titles.
3.  **Creating and Editing Charts**: The video walks through creating new visualizations, like bar charts, selecting X and Y axes, and grouping data. It shows how to stack items for better readability and customize tooltips for detailed hover information.
4.  **Color and Legend Management**: Users can manage default colors assigned to legend items, override them with custom colors, and rearrange legend items for better visual order.
5.  **Advanced Chart Features**: The video covers features like normalizing bar charts to percentages, combining different chart types (e.g., line and scatter), and the importance of starting with a bar chart before changing to other types to maintain stacking functionality.
6.  **Adding Descriptions and Parameters**: Users can add descriptions to visualizations and use parameters within these descriptions to dynamically update based on the data.
7.  **Final Steps**: Once a visualization is created, it can be added to any dashboard as a widget. Users can delete or modify visualizations as needed.

Overall, the video aims to equip users with the skills to effectively create, customize, and manage visualizations on Dune to better present their data.

‍

# Exploring Queries, Views, and Matviews - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

ExploringQueries,Views,andMatviews
==================================

Learn how to create, schedule, and optimize SQL queries on Dune with this in-depth guide covering query views, parameters, and materialized results for faster, smarter blockchain analysis.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•August 16, 2024•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

The video provides an introduction to creating, editing, and managing queries on the Dune platform. Key points include:

1.  **Basics of Queries**: The video starts with an overview of the query editor, explaining how to write and execute queries, view results, and use shortcuts. It introduces the SQL query text area, the run button, the query results section, and the table explorer for finding tables to query.
2.  **Forking and Creating Queries**: Users can fork existing queries or create new ones. Forking allows users to modify and save queries from others quickly.
3.  **Query Editor Components**: The query editor consists of three main parts: the SQL text area, the results section, and the data/table explorer. Users can run queries on different engines (free, medium, large) depending on their plan.
4.  **Scheduling and Parameters**: Users can schedule queries to run at specific intervals (e.g., daily, every 15 minutes) and add parameters for dynamic querying. The video covers handling parameters like text, dates, and lists, including specific tips and gotchas.
5.  **Query Views and Materialized Views**: The video explains how to create query views, which allow reusing queries within other queries, and materialized views, which store query results for faster access. It highlights the difference between query results and materialized view results.
6.  **Settings**: Users can manage query settings, such as changing the name, adding tags and descriptions, changing ownership, adjusting privacy settings, and archiving queries.

Overall, the video aims to familiarize users with the essential tools and features for effectively working with queries on Dune.

‍

# Dune - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

Dune
====

Today we are excited to announce that Dune Analytics, often referred to as just Dune, is now exactly that: Dune.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Research•April 19, 2022•2 min read

![@Fredrik Haga](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F52880ee8ffdf5c96f23bc355690443daba96f1f2-664x664.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Fredrik Haga](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fredrikhaga/)
Co-Founder & CEO at Dune

![Dune](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2037daaa9823c950a56015f15573f37c65aa9686-1200x675.png&w=3840&q=75)

Today we are excited to announce that Dune Analytics, often referred to as just Dune, is now exactly that: Dune. Not only that. You can now find us at dune.com where we’ll make web3 data accessible for millions of people, and empower a new generation of data Wizards.

When we founded Dune Analytics back in 2018 there were a couple of dozen smart contract products live on the Ethereum blockchain used by a few tech and finance geeks. We built a tool to let anyone easily analyze this activity. However, while our audience was pretty niche and technical back then, we didn’t want to name our product something overly technical like “Blockchain data technologies”. We wanted something more accessible, something neat and more playful. Dune was such a word. The lines of a dune resemble charts so there was a connection to what we’re building without making it too techy. As a bonus it’s also a pretty cool book. However, we needed to get an available domain so we became Dune Analytics and found a home at duneanalytics.com.

Since then the scope and reach of our industry has grown exponentially. It’s no longer just cryptocurrencies, it’s web3. It’s not just finance, it’s art, gaming, DAOs and beyond. It’s no longer just geeks messing around, there’s a massive consumer audience. It’s no longer just Ethereum layer 1, but a myriad of layers, chains and different approaches. Many crypto startups are now huge web3 companies, and even the established huge companies are embracing our world as well.

We supply data for all of these emerging use cases and users. But, we’re not just going to do this through enabling analytics dashboards. We are rapidly expanding our scope and offering. We will for instance soon launch the highly anticipated Dune API, which will create a living, breathing and performant API machine for the community and open up a whole new world of use cases. The data will flow.

Our mission is to make crypto data accessible and we’ve long known that eventually this day would come when _analytics_ is too limiting for describing what we’re up to. It’s a long and clunky word that is too cumbersome. Therefore, we’re today changing our name to simply _Dune_.

Oh and speaking of a large audience. You can now find the data flowing at _dune.com_. While we honestly quite liked our former home at dune.xyz we want to bring trusted web3 data to the _masses_. A neat 4 letter .com domain is the right place to do just that.

To us the new name of Dune and our new home at dune.com is a symbol of what crypto and we have grown into, but also a symbol of the ambitions we have for the future when we are going to make web3 data broadly accessible to millions of users.

The data must flow.

/Dune

# From Chain to Chains: Building a Superchain Data Team - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

FromChaintoChains:BuildingaSuperchainDataTeam
=============================================

Lizzy Wong (Optimism - OP Labs) discusses the challenges and strategies for building a superchain data team, sharing insights from the frontier of multichain analytics at DuneCon2024.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 3, 2024•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

At **DuneCon 2024**, Lizzy Wong, Head of Data at OP Labs, shared her expertise on building a superchain data team and navigating the challenges of multichain analytics. This engaging talk explored the intersection of data strategy, leadership, and the evolving world of blockchain ecosystems.

Key topics included:

*   The growth of Optimism's superchain and the increasing complexity of multichain data.
*   Why data is a strategic asset in the blockchain space, serving as a foundation for transparency, trust, and ecosystem growth.
*   Building and scaling high-performing, multidisciplinary data teams.
*   Data strategies, including the importance of trust, scalable infrastructure, and impactful ROI-driven metrics.
*   Insights into cross-chain analytics, interoperability, and tracking user behavior in a multichain world.

Lizzy also shared practical frameworks for prioritizing data initiatives, aligning metrics with business outcomes, and fostering collaboration across diverse teams. Whether you're a blockchain developer, data scientist, or enthusiast, this talk offered actionable strategies to thrive in the rapidly evolving landscape of Web3 analytics.

👉 Watch now to learn how data teams can drive innovation in the superchain era and subscribe for more insights from DuneCon 2024! 🚀

# Opening Keynote: Making Multichain Data Accessible - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

OpeningKeynote:MakingMultichainDataAccessible
=============================================

Fredrik Haga, CEO & Co-Founder of Dune, kicks off DuneCon2024 with major product announcements and insights on how Dune is making multichain data accessible, empowering data storytellers, and shaping the future of on-chain analytics.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 2, 2024•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

DuneCon 2024 was a dynamic showcase of innovation, celebrating Dune's mission to make crypto data accessible and pushing boundaries in the blockchain space. Over the past year, Dune has expanded from supporting 12 chains to an expected 93 by the end of 2024, including major additions like Stellar and Ronin, the largest gaming chain. The event brimmed with optimism as the team unveiled powerful new features, from multi-chain decoding and AI-driven query tools to expanded data sets like labeled blockchain addresses and industry-leading USD price coverage. Highlights included the introduction of Dune Metrics and the Dune Index, offering clearer insights into blockchain adoption through transaction fees, volumes, and counts. Developers were thrilled by the launch of Echo, a real-time multi-chain platform, and the announcement of Dune's acquisition of SMLXL, setting the stage for even faster, deeper, and more flexible blockchain analytics. The event’s energy and ambition underscored Dune’s leadership in shaping the future of blockchain data accessibility.

# Blueprint for Adoption: Paving the Path to a Blockchain World - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

BlueprintforAdoption:PavingthePathtoaBlockchainWorld
====================================================

Brandon Kumar (Layer3), Edwin Aoki (PayPal), and Amanda Cassatt (Serotonin) discuss strategies, challenges, and visions for driving blockchain adoption in this engaging #DuneCon2024 panel.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 3, 2024•2 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

At **#DuneCon2024**, **Brandon Kumar (Layer3)**, **Edwin Aoki (PayPal)**, and **Amanda Cassatt (Serotonin)** tackle the strategies, challenges, and opportunities for achieving mass blockchain adoption in this engaging panel discussion.

The panel begins by addressing the evolving role of institutions in blockchain adoption. Edwin highlights PayPal's role in bridging traditional finance and decentralized systems, emphasizing the importance of user choice between self-custody and institutional solutions. Brandon adds that the next decade will likely prioritize profit-driven applications, with less emphasis on asset ownership compared to earlier cycles.

Key topics explored include:

*   **Adoption Drivers**: From trading and speculation to stablecoins and financial inclusion, the panel outlines the diverse entry points for different user segments. They highlight how stablecoins address global payment inefficiencies, offering compelling use cases for businesses and unbanked populations.
*   **Barriers to Growth**: Regulatory ambiguity, compliance complexities, and entrenched habits in post-industrial markets are identified as significant hurdles. The panel underscores the need for better onboarding experiences and legal clarity to unlock institutional and retail adoption.
*   **Infrastructure Readiness**: While the consumer-facing blockchain infrastructure is deemed mature, institutional-scale solutions still require significant improvements. The panel notes the growing abstraction of blockchain interactions, with chat interfaces and simplified onboarding flows as pivotal trends.
*   **Future Opportunities**: The dominance of dollar-denominated assets and stablecoins as gateways to DeFi and broader blockchain usage is expected to persist. Emerging economies, motivated by the need for better financial tools, are identified as key growth regions.

The session concludes with a discussion on bridging decentralized and centralized systems, emphasizing the importance of flexibility and user-friendly designs to onboard the next billion users. With practical insights from leading experts, this panel provides a clear blueprint for driving blockchain adoption globally.

# Can I kick it? Dune at 5 - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

CanIkickit?Duneat5
==================

Building a startup is a walk on the wild side. Survival can’t be taken for granted.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

News•September 7, 2023•3 min read

![@Fredrik Haga](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F52880ee8ffdf5c96f23bc355690443daba96f1f2-664x664.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Fredrik Haga](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fredrikhaga/)
Co-Founder & CEO at Dune

![Can I kick it? Dune at 5](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F9c1d78f833472c8af8c312e0f2bea9bdfc0f097b-1200x675.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Can I kick it? Yes, Dune can! Building a startup is a walk on the wild side. Survival can’t be taken for granted. Dune was born September 2018, and today, I’m proud that we are alive and kicking with half a decade in the game. Here are some reflections as Dune turns 5 this week.

One of my most cherished possessions is this gold watch. My grandfather was a mechanical engineer fixing printing presses. This watch was a gift he received after working for the same company for more than 50 years - from the ages of 17 to 70!

![ width=](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fc30f4e4038ef8758e0979cc3fc913e9a304968bf-1200x1600.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

This treasured inheritance reminds me not of my love for hip hop with its flashy culture, or that we were paper rich at the height of the bull market. It reminds me of something much deeper, and that’s how long a long time really is. Doing something for a short period of time is easy, but doing something for a long period of time is really hard. Anybody can do something for a year or two. Doing something for multiple decades is an entirely different game.

Every cycle, the crypto space gets hyped to the moon and then dragged through the mud. Staying sane and playing the long game throughout these waves is hard, but necessary. If you make short term decisions during the bull market you will pay the price when the hype fades and realize that you’re unprepared. On the other hand, if you fold when times are hard you will not reap the benefits of the next world-changing wave this industry will unlock.

One thing I’ve found particularly important is to only work with long term and high integrity people. Across the board with your team, clients, investors and community. I was reminded of the importance of this at a board meeting we had at Union Square Ventures’ office last week. They know that world changing technologies can take a long time to play out, and can remain controversial for a long time. I’m very grateful for having investors that are truly believers and backers, not only through the good times but also the bad.

‍

![ width=](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F9be66dd8f293d35390e1baa7ad3e13319cd0c731-1600x1200.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

The USV office is filled with crypto manifestos and screens with NFTs.

![ width=](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F9e4db5610e967720db4922e7424a9184d41f9773-1200x1600.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

_My co-founder enjoying the view over Union Square_

When you run a business there’s a lot of talk and thinking around winning. However, there’s a very important prerequisite for winning that often gets overlooked: you’ve got to stay in the game to play the game. If you’re not in the game, you can’t win.

I often think about the [Lindy Effect](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lindy_effect)
: the idea that the longer something has existed the longer it is likely to keep existing. Each marginal day one stays alive doesn’t feel like any notable accomplishment when it’s day to day, but over time it really does matter. That Dune is now 5 years old, means we’re more likely to stick around for another 5 years and beyond. That is worthy of celebration.

The startup world is fast paced, and the crypto space even more so. A lot happens in a year, so 5 years feel like an eternity. At the same time, we’re only 10% of the way to earning and deserving my grandfather's gold watch. As an industry we’re building a new financial system from the ground up. It’s a massive undertaking that will inevitably take time. Interest rate paradigms, investor interest, regulators and public sentiment will come and go. It’s a fool’s errand to think anything will be an overnight success. But if we keep working hard, one day at a time and stay alive, we will eventually accomplish great things.

Being long term is easy to say, but hard to do. Half a decade of making crypto data accessible is a big milestone for Dune, us as founders, the whole team, investors, community, partners and customers. This journey truly is a walk on the wild side but we’re alive and kicking! Here’s to kicking it another 5 years and beyond, happy birthday Dune!

/Fredrik

‍

PS. One point to you if you realized that “Can I kick it” is a homage to the legendary Tribe Called Quest song :)

PPS. Bonus points if you caught that that song samples Lou Reed’s Walk on the Wild Side ;)

Doo do doo do doo do do doo...

‍

# Decoding Contracts - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

DecodingContracts
=================

Learn how to decode smart contracts on Dune to unlock readable blockchain data, understand proxies and factories, and build powerful queries from decoded tables.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•August 16, 2024•2 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

The video provides a detailed overview of the contract decoding process on Dune, which is essential for working with complex blockchain data. Key points include:

1.  **Accessing the Decoding Page**: Users can submit contracts for decoding through the Dune platform. This process is documented in Dune’s official documentation.
2.  **Purpose of Decoding**: Decoding contracts simplifies the raw data, which often appears in hexadecimal and requires complex functions to interpret. Decoding makes it easier to work with this data by translating it into more accessible tables.
3.  **Submitting a Contract**: Users can submit a contract for decoding by entering the contract address into the submission page. If the contract has already been decoded, they can access the related tables directly. When submitting, users should indicate if the contract is a proxy or a factory, and if it’s a proxy, they should submit the implementation address.
4.  **Checking Decoding Status**: Users can monitor the status of their contract submissions under their user settings. Decoding typically takes a full day, after which the tables become available for querying.
5.  **Understanding Proxies and Factories**: The video explains how to identify if a contract is a proxy or a factory by checking certain indicators on Etherscan, such as whether a contract has a "read/write proxy" option or if the contract was deployed by another contract. These distinctions are crucial for accurate data decoding.
6.  **Querying Decoded Tables**: Once a contract is decoded, users can query the related tables directly. If the data appears incomplete shortly after decoding, it might still be backfilling, and users should allow some time for the process to complete.

Overall, the video guides users through the essential steps of submitting and working with decoded contracts on Dune, emphasizing the importance of understanding proxies, factories, and the decoding process to effectively manage and query blockchain data.

# About Dune | Dune

Let's make crypto data accessible!
==================================

We're busy building a unique product that integrates a cutting-edge data platform, advanced analytics tools, and a thriving creator community within the world of web3.

[Contact us](https://dune.com/contact)
[Careers](https://dune.com/careers)

#### Backed by the best

Our mission

We're a rapidly expanding team dedicated to crafting a beloved product that embodies the principles of open finance within the data layer.

2018

Founded

50+

Employees

$80m

Raised

![Dune team photo](https://dune.com/assets/Dune_Team.webp?dpl=dpl_BSRJYSM9KHdvNzmZSqwZVa8xgNS1)

Our story so far...
-------------------

2018

Foundation

Fredrik and Mats founded Dune in Oslo. They were cleaning up Ethereum data to one paying customer. After months of hard work, rejections, and no salary, a pre-seed round was raised.

2020

DeFi summer

Still only two founders with a big mission: raising a seed round. The bear market turned into DeFi summer, and things were looking up.

2021

Series A and growth

Dune hired its first full time employees and started rebuilding the Dune app from scratch. It gained traction in and outside crypto Twitter, securing a Series A, and the team grew rapidly.

2022

Unicorn status

Even the founders were surprised, yet Dune secured a Series B, propelling Dune to unicorn status with just 16 employees. Since then, the team has tripled, and Dune hosted its first event in Berlin (DuneCon).

2023

Groundbreaking products

The adoption of Dune in the web3 industry continues to grow exponentially. Dune released many groundbreaking products like Dune AI and Data Hub and officially launched its Dune Enterprise solution.

2024

From idea to impact

Dune became the place where onchain teams ship insights together. Dune grew its enterprise footprint and invested in reliability and speed so analysts and builders could go from idea to impact faster.

2025

From insights to impact

Dune is rolling out connectors so enterprise customers can access Dune's powerful ecosystem through any workflow. DuneCon returns in Buenos Aires, bringing builders, analysts, and partners together to shape the future of onchain data.

# Datashare | Dune

Datashare

Query onchain data directly in your data warehouse
==================================================

Get unprecedented access to the most comprehensive blockchain datasets in crypto.

Schedule a Demo

Onchain data, in your environment
---------------------------------

### No data plumbing

Easily query directly where you do your data work

### Fully customizable

Scale and use your own compute, join with your offchain data as you see fit

### White-glove support

Our team can find or build the right dataset for you

How it works
------------

Data warehouse integration

Access Dune data in your own environment with connection to Snowflake, BigQuery, and Databricks in any region.

Cross-region replication

Data automatically synced across multiple regions for low-latency access wherever you operate.

Consolidate data sources

Combine onchain and offchain data in your environment.

Historical data access

Feed historical blockchain data into your apps and workflows, including Solana.

Access comprehensive trading data and market intelligence directly in your analytics environment.

# Dune's Vision - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

Dune'sVision
============

Our mission is to make crypto data accessible. What does the world look like when we get there? Here’s our take.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Team•December 13, 2022•3 min read

![@Fredrik Haga](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F52880ee8ffdf5c96f23bc355690443daba96f1f2-664x664.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Fredrik Haga](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fredrikhaga/)
Co-Founder & CEO at Dune

![Dune's Vision](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fd7c7d23d7a226806d6246fb7625144820c12ed02-1200x675.png&w=3840&q=75)

We believe that in the future every business, and every _person_, will rely on blockchains.

The internet showed us the power of an open, global system for information sharing. It accelerated human progress faster and wider than the previous closed, top-down systems where corporations, institutions, and governments dictated who could participate.

Crypto _extends_ the internet, transforming it from an information system on traditional economic rails into a full-blown native economy. Now anyone can build and interact with value globally. Open infrastructure isn’t the only factor that made the internet so powerful though. Every massive internet success leveraged an equally massive amount of _data_.

On the internet, and in the wider world in general, valuable data has traditionally been a closed, tightly gatekept resource. Only insiders with enough money or pull at the right megacorp could access, understand and build with it. Not anymore. Blockchains represent the world’s first _public by default_, ever-expanding datasets. This is a paradigm shift.

In Web2, interactions between user and application were a guarded secret. On public blockchains though, every data point drives an _explosion_ of stakeholders and analysts. The interaction is accessible to _anyone_ - builders, users, DAOs, communities, competitors, corporations, regulators, and investors of all shapes and sizes.

For the first time we can collaborate and learn with data not only within teams or organizations, but together as a global community. Sharing and collaboration become the default, the starting point rather than an afterthought.

You can study how users interact with top DEXes or lending protocols, and build even better versions. You can evaluate the traction of new networks yourself and decide whether to invest money or resources in it. You can get into the nuts and bolts of how entire crypto ecosystems function, and make your own mind up on their value and utility. Open data doesn’t care about your credentials, job, wealth, background or location - _you_ can learn anything you like, and contribute to humanity's knowledge of the on-chain world.

If Dune succeeds, the world has a collective homepage for understanding on-chain activity. Instead of falling prey to dubious narratives and hype, the curious can check the chain and make informed decisions based on hard data. We want to give anyone with an internet connection _better_ access to data than the big tech engineers and hedge fund managers of the legacy world could have ever dreamt of. Any insight you need - whether an analysis of traction across a category, a real time balance sheet, user engagement and retention, or a deep risk analysis - will be accessible to anyone with some SQL skill or the motivation to acquire it.

We also want to build Dune to be as flexible as possible, so you can create, fork and remix the work of others. Forget the opaque quarterly PDFs, excel sheets and databases inside the walled gardens of institutions. Instead of lurking in the shadows - risks, opportunities, and innovation will be illuminated in Dune dashboards by the light of the Wizard community’s wands.

Dune will also make it easy to incorporate data _into_ your applications and workflows without worrying about the underlying infrastructure. When the builders of the world have a public dataset and know how to use it, magic will happen.

‍_Anyone_ will be able to use crypto data. To build a business. To build a product. To join data with on-chain activity. To power an app with AI training data. To distribute rewards to valuable users. To build better risk models. To build alerts & engagement tools for any use case. The possibilities will be endless.

No longer will you need to be an insider to launch, gain traction and scale. Creativity will be the only limitation. Dune will let you easily build with and integrate the best data in the world, collectively curated and maintained by thousands of wizards working together.

Innovation is driven by a feedback loop between building and learning. When the loop spins faster, human progress accelerates. At Dune we want to make it spin faster by transforming blockchain data from a theoretical possibility to a practical reality. We want to empower analysts and wizards to come together as a super-organism that both fuels and feeds on the growth of the open economy.

If we are successful, any curious mind can use Dune to learn, explore and build the future.

# Eyes on Asia: Does On-chain Data Reveal the Shift? - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

EyesonAsia:DoesOn-chainDataRevealtheShift?
==========================================

Daniel Kim (Tiger Research) examines whether on-chain data highlights a growing shift toward Asia, offering key insights and trends at #DuneCon2024.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 3, 2024•2 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

At **DuneCon 2024**, **Daniel Kim** of **Tiger Research** delivered a compelling presentation exploring whether on-chain data signals a growing shift toward Asia's dominance in the Web3 and blockchain space.

### Key Takeaways:

1.  **Why Asia Matters:**
2.  **Crypto Leadership:**58% of global crypto users are in Asia.Countries like **Japan** and **Korea** rank second and fourth in crypto trading volumes by fiat currency.Asia is home to 51% of global GitHub developers.
3.  **Consumer-Centric Digital Culture:**
    *   Asian markets lead in **consumer applications** like payment, healthcare, and super apps, with examples including **WeChat, KakaoTalk**, and **LINE**.
    *   Super apps provide seamless integration of daily activities, making them ideal platforms for Web3 adoption.
4.  **On-Chain Data Insights:**
5.  **Stablecoins:**Significant growth in local currency-denominated stablecoins across Asia, particularly in Singapore (XSGD) and Indonesia (XIDR).**Smart Contracts:**Asia now matches other regions in Ethereum mainnet smart contract deployment, highlighting increased developer activity.
6.  **Behavioral Shifts in DeFi:**
    *   Asian activity on Uniswap has grown steadily, with a noticeable shift from **shark investors** (2020) to **retail investors** (2024).
    *   This indicates increased adoption by everyday users moving from centralized to decentralized exchanges.
7.  **Web3 Social Trends:**
    *   Asian languages like Thai, Vietnamese, and Korean are increasingly dominant on platforms like **Forecaster**.
    *   Engagement rates and post volumes from Asian users have surpassed those of North America and Europe since March 2023.
8.  **Leverage Asia’s Momentum:**  
    Asia is driving Web3's growth with its unique blend of young demographics, digital adoption, and economic expansion.
9.  **Adapt Strategies Locally:**
    *   Asia’s diversity demands tailored approaches for each market.
    *   Work with local partners to navigate cultural nuances and regulatory landscapes.

👉 **Watch this session now** to uncover actionable insights and strategies for tapping into Asia’s thriving Web3 ecosystem! 🚀

‍

# Execution Through Ownership - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

ExecutionThroughOwnership
=========================

We believe in efficient execution through clear ownership. Here’s what we mean by that and how we do it at Dune.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

News•July 14, 2022•4 min read

![@Fredrik Haga](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F52880ee8ffdf5c96f23bc355690443daba96f1f2-664x664.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Fredrik Haga](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fredrikhaga/)
Co-Founder & CEO at Dune

![Execution Through Ownership](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F91819d0d0b65d9abc6cf2d6077cc859908767f04-1200x1200.png&w=3840&q=75)

These are hard times in the crypto space, and in the startup world broadly. Both funding and traction is harder to obtain than before. That means it’s incredibly important to ensure one executes efficiently with the people you have on the team. Mats and I ran Dune as a two person operation for over 2 years and we did a unicorn round at only 16 employees, so seemingly we’re somewhat efficient executors. We believe in efficient execution through clear ownership. Here’s what we mean by that and how we do it at Dune.

In many workplaces there is this cozy idea that the more people you have on a problem the better. Gather the troops around the campfire and bring out the post-it’s: “let’s brainstorm”. Not at Dune. One of our values at Dune is ownership. We always strive to be explicit and make it clear which exact person is responsible for a problem we’re trying to solve. We think this is key to ensure quality of execution, speed and empowerment of each team member.

We think bringing in a ton of folks and opinions early in the process of creating something is usually incredibly time consuming and inefficient. Early on there’s a lot of ambiguity and many potentially viable paths one could go down to solve a problem. In general people are pretty good at just having an opinion and saying something when they are asked to do so. That does not mean that the specific insight that comes out is too useful; just look at most conference panels.

So one might explore X paths with Y team members and spend Z time on each, while the solution can only be one path. So if you have many potential paths, involve many people and they all spend some time on it; X \* Y \* Z = way too much time spent across the company.

Assuming there are many very important problems to solve at all times, we think this is an undesirable use of resources. Instead, once a problem is identified we give a single person full ownership and trust them to run with it. That does not mean that we can’t or won’t work together on many problems, it just means that the responsible person explores the most viable solutions and loops in others when needed for their feedback, pushback or contribution to the project.

Once a clear proposal is formed others can give feedback and scrutinize the proposal. It’s way easier to work efficiently as a group when there’s something specific to discuss. In our equation above the potential solutions (X) are reduced to only the most viable ones and the number of team members (Y) can likely be reduced as you have a better sense of who can give useful feedback. Since there’s a more specific thing to discuss, time per team member (Z) will also go down, or at least be concentrated on viable solutions.

![ width=](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F9687d5726a492a58ffb0bc7891e1e6bd408cee14-1074x876.png&w=3840&q=75)

The fewer people involved early, the faster the execution. If you’re confident that you have the right solution you can go far alone. If you are not, you can loop in others earlier. There can be many reasons for looping in people earlier or later. For more ambiguous and complicated projects asking for feedback when you are only 30% done could make sense. For projects that are more defined and easier to solve, maybe one person can define an 80% solution before it’s shared or even execute entirely on their own. A more junior team member might bring in others earlier, while a more senior will go further alone. Maybe there are a lot of dependencies and that means you need to involve more people early.

The key here is that we trust the owner of each problem to loop people in when they are the most useful. This does not mean that people won’t be working together as a team. It just means whenever many people are involved we’re ensuring that what is asked from them is clear, and their time is well spent. You’re not being asked to just “say something”, you are asked to review specific parts of a specific proposal for a specific reason.

At Dune the way we usually go about the above process for bigger efforts is via written design documents in Google Docs. Problems to be solved typically arise from company OKRs or user requests. After we’ve defined a problem to be solved the owner lays out the scope of the document, the relevant background information, proposed solutions and tradeoffs made. Whenever the owner deems it relevant the other team members can comment and give feedback and approval in the document. There are usually no meetings needed to do this. It’s all living in docs where one can discuss and decide asynchronously. The process can take one iteration in a day or several months depending on the complexity of the problem at hand.

Previously in my career I’ve seen a lot of “work” being done, but when there was a lack of speed and quality of execution there was only endless spider mans pointing fingers in all other directions. No-one dared to say “you are going to be responsible here and own this”. While being an assigned owner can be a bit intimidating it’s also incredibly empowering and liberating for each team member. When someone is responsible they have the freedom to move forward and responsibility to create lasting solutions. What better feeling than knowing that this is _my responsibility_? I can pull on my prefered superhero suit, roll up my sleeves and have the power and ability to make this happen faster and better than anyone thought was possible, lfg!

🫡

# We've raised a $69,420,000 Series B led by Coatue - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

We'veraiseda$69,420,000SeriesBledbyCoatue
=========================================

We’re excited to announce that we’ve raised $69,420,000 in a Series B round led by Coatue to realize our mission of making crypto data accessible.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

News•February 2, 2022•3 min read

![@Fredrik Haga](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F52880ee8ffdf5c96f23bc355690443daba96f1f2-664x664.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Fredrik Haga](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fredrikhaga/)
Co-Founder & CEO at Dune

![We've raised a $69,420,000 Series B led by Coatue](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fd3ace343bc91eb4733e0bb932e5f4d87bbeda622-2000x1125.png&w=3840&q=75)

We’re excited to announce that we’ve raised $69 420 000 in a Series B round led by Coatue to realize our mission of making crypto data accessible. With this new funding we will educate, reward and empower a new generation of web3 data analysts aka Dune Wizards.

_We reached unicorn status at only 16 employees and are growing quickly so if you want to have one of the highest impact jobs on the planet:_ [dune.xyz/careers](https://dune.xyz/careers)

Blockchains are globally shared public backends where thousands of apps and millions of transactions create an ocean of data every day. As more and more financial activity moves to crypto, structuring and providing insights into this open data represents an opportunity that is many times bigger than any data business or product the world has seen to date. Some say Dune could become “as big as TradFi data tools”, but we think open blockchain data is a much bigger opportunity than siloed and paywalled data tools from the past. The revolution will not be reported quarterly.

There’s a ton of insights in crypto data, but without skilled and incentivized analysts coupled with great tooling this information will remain hidden beneath the surface. With this financing we’re doubling down on our mission to make crypto data accessible and are committed to bringing a million Dune Wizards to web3. There are mainly three ways in which we will do this:

**Educate** - With Dune anyone can access blockchain data for free. But that’s only step one, truly valuable insights come when you can dissect that data. As blockchains keep scaling to new users and use cases there’s going to be an ever increasing need for understanding the data. With this new funding we’ll invest heavily in educational resources to ensure that _anyone_ can learn the valuable skills needed to analyze blockchain data, even users without technical backgrounds.

**Reward** - Historically, user generated content has not been rewarded, but this time it’s different. There are already many individuals and groups getting paid for their Dune creations. While we have no plans to issue a token, we’ll embrace the web3 toolkit to ensure that Wizards adding value to the community will get paid accordingly, as they should. With the open data opportunity at hand we’re convinced that many Dune Wizards will earn more than Wall Street analysts have done previously, all the while also having way more fun.

**Empower** - We will make Dune the most convenient and powerful data tool on the planet. A completely new Dune query engine will soon be live. It will dramatically speed up query execution time, allow us to support all major blockchains, and let Wizards analyze across all of them _in one single query_. Users will be able to bring their own data, both public and private, into Dune. Our application will support more features for teams and become even more collaborative. Last but not least we’ll ship one of our most requested features ever: the Dune API! This massive new product will change the crypto data stack forever, creating a living, breathing and performant community-driven API to enable boundary-less research, building, tinkering, trading and beyond.

Crypto prices go up and down, and for the first two years of Dune’s existence prices were down. We kept building with high pace and conviction for the community regardless. This financing will allow us to build out the Dune team, product and community aggressively to make crypto data accessible, even through a sustained bear market.

Oh and yes to make all of this happen we are hiring big time. We’ve reached unicorn status at only 16 employees, so if you want to have one of the highest impact jobs on the planet come join our rapidly growing team of galaxy brains: [dune.com/careers](https://dune.xyz/careers)

If you want to start your crypto data journey and become a Dune Wizard today join our [Discord](https://discord.com/invite/ErrzwBz)
 and check out all the educational material in our [docs](https://docs.dune.xyz/)
. Dune Wizards are in high demand, and no prior knowledge or coding experience is required.

The data must flow.

/Dune Founders Fredrik and Mats

# Dune Acquires smlXL to Strengthen Real-Time Blockchain Data Capabilities - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

DuneAcquiressmlXLtoStrengthenReal-TimeBlockchainDataCapabilities
================================================================

We’re thrilled to welcome smlXL to the Dune family
--------------------------------------------------

News•November 11, 2024•2 min read

data

dune-api

dune-team

real-time-data

scaling-solution

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

![Dune Acquires smlXL to Strengthen Real-Time Blockchain Data Capabilities](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F7104fd5b11e3cc3cc95772a910657e0639c80b90-1200x675.png&w=3840&q=75)

At Dune, our mission is to make blockchain data accessible and actionable for everyone. Today, we’re excited to announce the acquisition of [smlXL](https://smlxl.io/)
, a company at the forefront of real-time blockchain data simulation and analysis. This move marks a significant step forward in our efforts to provide developers with faster, deeper, and more flexible access to on-chain data.

Why smlXL?
----------

Founded in 2021 by Dor Levi, smlXL has developed tools that enable real-time blockchain transaction simulation. Their flagship product, [sim Studio](http://sim.io/)
, allows developers to simulate and replay transactions in real time, offering unparalleled insights into blockchain activity. smlXL also built a proprietary Ethereum node, **iEVM**, which leverages a unique data format to deliver rapid, detailed blockchain data.

With smlXL’s technology, Dune will enhance its real-time data infrastructure, providing developers with even more robust tools for querying, analyzing, and leveraging blockchain data.

> “
> 
> “smlXL has built a game-changing new way of getting flexible real-time data from the EVM. Together we will make it 10x easier for developers to build with onchain data” said Fredrik Haga, Co-founder & CEO of Dune.

> “
> 
> "We're thrilled to join Dune. Our shared mission of making blockchain data more accessible creates a perfect match. Together, we're building the definitive destination for all blockchain data needs." — Dor Levi, CEO & Founder at smlXL

What This Means for Developers
------------------------------

With smlXL’s integration, Dune will be able to offer:

*   [Real-Time API](https://community.dune.com/blog/introducing-echo-dunes-new-multichain-real-time-developer-platform)
     **Enhancements**: Faster access to blockchain data, enabling developers to build and improve applications more efficiently.
*   **Multichain Support**: Seamless access to data across multiple blockchains, expanding the scope and utility of Dune’s platform.
*   **Scalable Solutions**: New tools to handle large-scale data needs, whether for querying, simulation, or analytics.

This acquisition accelerates Dune’s ability to support developers in building data-driven applications that push the boundaries of what’s possible in the blockchain space.

We’re thrilled to welcome smlXL to the Dune family and look forward to building the future of blockchain data together.

For more details on smlXL and their products, visit [sim.io](https://sim.io/)
. Learn about [Echo](https://dune.com/blog/introducing-echo-dunes-new-multichain-real-time-developer-platform)
, Dune’s new multichain real-time developer platform.

# Exploring and Contributing to Spellbook (Curated Tables) - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

ExploringandContributingtoSpellbook(CuratedTables)
==================================================

Master Dune’s Spellbook to build, explore, and contribute to curated blockchain datasets — the foundation for advanced Web3 data modeling and analytics.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•August 16, 2024•2 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

The video provides a comprehensive overview of Spellbook, Dune's community-driven DBT (Data Build Tool) platform used for managing and building data models, specifically curated datasets like Dex trades and other blockchain-related data.

### Key Points:

1.  **Introduction to Spellbook**:
    *   Spellbook is the platform where curated data like Dex trades, lending labels, and addresses are managed.
    *   It utilizes DBT, which helps manage the execution and lineage of tables or queries, organizing them into a directed acyclic graph (DAG) to show dependencies.
2.  **Understanding Lineage**:
    *   Lineage in Spellbook shows how different tables are connected and built from one another.
    *   The video explains how to navigate and understand these lineages, using the example of Dex trades, and how data flows from raw tables to more refined datasets.
3.  **Navigating and Searching in Spellbook**:
    *   Users can search and navigate through different models, macros, and sub-projects within Spellbook to understand how data is structured and connected.
    *   The video walks through examples of how to find specific tables, understand their lineage, and trace back to their source models.
4.  **Contributing to Spellbook**:
    *   The video demonstrates how to set up a local environment for Spellbook, contribute by fixing bugs or adding new tables, and navigate the PR (Pull Request) process.
    *   It shows a practical example of identifying an issue with a table (missing revenue data for a rollup), locating the source of the problem, and submitting a fix.
5.  **Collaborating and Staying Updated**:
    *   Users are encouraged to report issues, request new features, or contribute to discussions within Spellbook.
    *   The video highlights the importance of collaboration and how staying engaged with the latest changes in Spellbook can rapidly enhance a user’s expertise in the field.
6.  **Becoming an Expert**:
    *   By diving deep into Spellbook, users can quickly advance to becoming top experts in data engineering and analysis within the Dune ecosystem.
    *   The video concludes by encouraging users to explore and contribute, positioning Spellbook as the foundational layer beneath the more visible aspects of Dune’s data infrastructure.

Overall, the video aims to empower users to navigate, understand, and contribute to Spellbook, enhancing their skills in managing and building complex data models within the Dune ecosystem.

‍

# Meet the team: Alanna - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

Meettheteam:Alanna
==================

A Senior Product Designer’s journey from studying human behavior to crafting cutting-edge blockchain tools—blending creativity, data, and teamwork at Dune!
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Team•March 28, 2025•3 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

![Meet the team: Alanna](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fbc5c4a91e517ae04345cfc72aa42dc8a70a5b619-1200x675.png&w=3840&q=75)

Tell us about what you do at Dune?
----------------------------------

I’m a product designer at Dune. More specifically, I joined with the rest of the smlXL team following the acquisition in January. At smlXL, we built some really powerful tools to help developers create real-time products using on-chain data. Now, I’m focused on figuring out how to integrate what we built at smlXL into Dune’s mission of making crypto data accessible — and the design questions that come with that have been super interesting. It’s an exciting time to be working on design at Dune!

Since we’re fully remote, my mornings are usually filled with brainstorming sessions with the design team on larger initiatives, syncing with engineering on a product we’re building, or conducting user research. Thankfully, we’re not overloaded with meetings, so I usually have plenty of time in the afternoons for focused design work.

How did you get into design?
----------------------------

When I started college, I was convinced I wanted to be a psychologist (spoiler: I was very wrong). What I really enjoyed was learning about human behavior and figuring out how people think. That eventually led me to design — the perfect blend of human behavior, creativity, technical complexity, and business acumen. Once I found it, I was hooked.

I started my career at IBM’s iX design studio, consulting for large government agencies. That’s where I realized I was drawn to data-heavy, system design products and complex people problems. Eventually, this led me to Chainalysis, my first experience in the blockchain space. I was drawn to the scale and ambiguity of the problems they were solving, especially around using crowdsourced data to help investigators solve cases more efficiently. Funny enough, I worked closely with Hannah Curtis there — and now we get to work together again at Dune!

After Chainalysis, I joined smlXL as the founding product designer. The technical complexity of what we were building was equal parts intimidating and exciting, but enabling others to build products they wouldn’t have been able to create otherwise was incredibly rewarding. Now, being part of the Dune team and helping merge two amazing products has been an incredible experience.

What has been your most memorable Dune moment?
----------------------------------------------

Definitely Dunecon in Thailand this past year. Before the smlXL team officially joined Dune, we flew to Bangkok to meet everyone and take part in Dunecon. There was this incredible energy as the two teams got to know each other while also pulling off an amazing event. I was blown away by how welcoming the Dune team was and how aligned we all were in terms of values — it made me feel really confident about joining.

![ width=](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F8608a67617c7a317d8c81f98c49ad2c7c2e2714f-600x800.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

From our offsite in Paris, a group of us climbed the Eiffel Tower (yes- we did the stairs, not the elevator!)

‍What does getting to the heart of it mean to you in practice?
--------------------------------------------------------------

At early-stage companies working on complex and ambitious problems, communication is everything. To me, getting to the heart of it means facing challenging problems head-on as a team — breaking them down together and working through them openly. What I really value about Dune is the shared sense of humility and curiosity — it makes those tough conversations easier and leads to better outcomes and stronger relationships.

‍

Connect with Alanna:[‍](https://alannafischer.com/)
