# Dune Digest 019 - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

DuneDigest019
=============

News•July 23, 2025•3 min read

[Solanasolana](https://dune.com/blockchains/solana)

![@Filippo Armani](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Ff9dd54cabfaa63ae0dda792b5072e44d5b9b9971-800x800.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Filippo Armani](https://www.linkedin.com/in/filarm/)
Data Content Creator at Dune

![Dune Digest 019](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fc2353b4bdf63dda862180746fcd1b6e7467ff998-1200x675.png&w=3840&q=75)

[Plasma Launches $XPL Sale After $1B+ Deposits](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VVDzhS2DNmhfW7s0Dk_65fPQcW8kTzPf5z9JQBN54gmPq3qn9qW7lCdLW6lZ3lhW1WHV4713_kB8W13CH2c12BjrdW1xTm1T3bSNNvW65TPZZ4Nbk20W4-nj9k1NNKwfW7bPP4T2Nz5YLW8PKSBw5SZQR-W5Tm-D24jwMptW3WmKf021-grKW2s6krl3S3J1LW7JdTBr2GKKGRVPXc4q3gTldzW169y-l8Bk3g1W5DRLzG2qXH1qW3ggQFX9d70z7N4DhFJ4m19YVW7rPMnc2F94_9W23TYw69hRWBDN3YByjCXQwzYW13dQCd7mxyvqW238wKW2wJX88N54yFcFV6m3YW9hcKpz2cwSL0W1rlsQy2kpcCwdz0LwY04)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This week Plasma, a new **L1 purpose-built for stablecoins**, held its **$XPL public sale** powered by Sonar, the token sale infrastructure from Echo. The process began with a two-phase deposit window on Ethereum, where **users locked over $1B in stablecoins** —58% USDC, 40% USDT, and the rest in DAI and USDS. A total of **2,930 addresses participated**, with an **average deposit of $341K**; the largest single deposit reached $49.9M. **The token sale** [went live on July 17](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VVDzhS2DNmhfW7s0Dk_65fPQcW8kTzPf5z9JQBN54gmQ03qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3p-W7FpR8X3_cJkwVTMr1c6RdbvdN94j_Qm2DymzW7hV8HV2qK-ZbV9dy3T8_0_j_Vgc2QG5BLwckMGgMwYMMZjCW1VXK5S7BsTNCW5b4sj67XQPzqW3wpKHG7L7t0VW1BNzjT6hnR-zW7J7Bzs4SZgX-W2ByB4N4XRYJYW3FcVYJ13T9wmW48spk77dVBwxW7Rcx-d76VbQWW5pVn8R374sMfMVGJKffWjMRW3sbpZg8R85PSN85chvymczWTW1Ct3xy1XkDGlN5KCFKSyrc8LVzTkll4gsZrYW2Wm22P8-gMcwW7SRbSp1V50FzW4sF6h74mF2WwW2zx0jK3SFf5rW8l-dMf3LqqByf28FrSq04)
, **offering $50M of XPL at a $500M FDV**. So far, **over $35M has been committed** from **more than 1,000 users**, averaging over $33K per commitment. All vault deposits will be bridged to Plasma’s Mainnet Beta, where participants can withdraw funds and claim tokens. The structure—based on time-weighted deposits and onchain transparency—reflects Plasma’s ambition to build a stablecoin-native financial system with fairer and more programmable capital formation.

![Chart (7)-2](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fafebcb3eeeb8a466c49529291439c14e526ad830-1120x701.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

[Morpho Hits $8B+ in Total Deposits](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VVDzhS2DNmhfW7s0Dk_65fPQcW8kTzPf5z9JQBN54gmPK3qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3l1W4x7Vm51NhK68W8DlSLs7yFQTsW5THn1z21dH1mW35nYVb4JpTF1W2Dkc811gc7_BW93xM0p7F2rlYW4w750M7bP0x5W1L-2-K6-kKw_W2wSk5c9k5g-6W3FSlC34t9B3fVwz5Bt1JlgxGW870-C19dbSY3N4p5Kr3Mk6sPW2LGYrq2gnDrdVk2M8y7j5Vl7W98mX0b2FDV61W2mxhRw6hVq9VN1Ylhn1vG91sW5XYYd21YZhDHVxKzBq1Hd_4WW5v216H2kLlSbW3pTBJJ6WWMf2N1BVfj6FpfNfVCdVy022CzJWN32ylnwwqLZGVrHrTG8Td9s3f83PS1q04)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Morpho’s growth shows no signs of slowing down. This week, the protocol surpassed **$8B in total deposits**, fueled by cross-chain expansion and product innovation. **Katana**, launched just a few weeks ago, already became the fourth-largest chain by lending deposits with over $200M. It also launched on **Unichain**, claiming the top spot there, and [expanded](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VVDzhS2DNmhfW7s0Dk_65fPQcW8kTzPf5z9JQBN54gmQ03qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3ngW1mSx013J744SN3kny_8tzrz8V_lvyn3-T6tkW3L2CHT11xvWZW4fpkC14BJ29tW8t1LVZ1lP4N-W1xTV_w7LnxnCN5f19rWgN2P-N8M0-qN_cNgXW43TFDV7H2qZ8W13z78Q67hsVgW6dbGcj2yhQ9sW4jk1wb3Z4VkXW7hffJn6l7lpfW5rJMxk60FSKXN8J07D-8mFVVW1L5Y1w4r-VQDW6S9PkF7wBWbxW5_wkXt4NGkhWMTF7TnZ2QqyW1W-hZv64YvX8W5SkgMd5jthJdW1SP06n9clKs0W3tV1Cy6jqQJSW4rbblK3fx0WrW2Hhcdn22QcWKW6wsgFW1xlcq5N2nNKTWLgJL2f87tdpv04)
 to TON via **TAC**. With Morpho V2’s intent-based lending gaining traction, the protocol is increasingly cementing itself as the backbone of efficient, permissionless lending across the modular DeFi stack.

![Chart (5)-3](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F3be8150a9119e96ade81e6de0ead4f2c251832c5-1120x744.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

[Mass Rises in the Race for Mobile-First DeFi](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VVDzhS2DNmhfW7s0Dk_65fPQcW8kTzPf5z9JQBN54gmPK3qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3mtVvmqXq1M6R_MW4MFLLC3CRFmJW15gPg210qVNPW2ltXxX6SPLdmW8GYTZy8NWYVkVc9w9f7nVs1xW9d0Mxv75cQ0PW4k1wmH7ThZlwW4p9t598lRsx3W80g9C63Nvv9QW6FqF9C2ytp47W4kMSxH1-q1SbW3ZQNCx5dqdVnN6F10d4sBTyXW4lycM53SnmRXVFzd4y7DZTxVW8VQLSm82rKbXV6dknQ7z6TfqW7NFGZt1VsDvkN4m_-5F4TbdWW3t5JNG3DlL47W6-SVH-3mXBRWV884wh6Jbd2lW5zBv7s7HdW-0VR7Q2Q5FkK84W16YDhF8QnVwQf6LyTBq04)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mass, a **mobile-first DeFi app**, is gaining attention as part of the broader shift toward simplified onchain trading experiences. The app supports **perps trading via Hyperliquid** (integrated on July 2), **tokenized US equities** [via Dinari](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VVDzhS2DNmhfW7s0Dk_65fPQcW8kTzPf5z9JQBN54gmPK3qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3phW5vY3KY775S8HW3M0rgh1DfF7WV3tCTc8wxQBvVpj5YP1_hjtTW7zN_9C5Kh1CKW1s2C767l2SgKW9kP-4N81gnWsN1y3gzFFXvXqV7rNJD2fyPbYW83fM0Z5W78shW56YxtS40NfvmW6cBc_L1t3zmpW3qYTZF5bN_1gVSNzKM5lvv-_W2T1FPR4BSqpdW6Cnjp77PdYYhW9htN0f42mfTDW7LC-ph9hZ1lLN2SJwDS4b8JNN776C7C6ksMgW3D-jpN8W51Q9W3QV6974DpRlWW4KJNMy34f7szW5Jt4vc12SsLrN2_CPGx1W_JGW8FhTY12S-WBxf4mPSqM04)
, and **credit markets through Aave**, all accessible through a gasless, mobile interface. As of mid-July, the app has surpassed **1.3K unique users**, with 470+ active this month, mostly on Base and Arbitrum, and has processed over **22,000 transactions**. **Cumulative fees exceeded $76K**, with an all-time high of $5K in a single day. Mass has been frequently cited in discussions around the “Hyperliquid mobile app arms race,” alongside apps like Phantom and Lootbase for its accessible UX and integrated DeFi stack. While still early, the recent growth reflects growing interest in mobile-native, retail-focused DeFi tooling.

![png (4)-1](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F96747fecb30f383cc85830097e10e3765c47e2c3-1006x648.png&w=3840&q=75)

[Symphony Hits $40M Total Volume as Sei Activity Surges](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VVDzhS2DNmhfW7s0Dk_65fPQcW8kTzPf5z9JQBN54gmPK3qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3nZW8BLrSt2X1XFpN2Y32VS-TFq4W21k9qm7xz3G1VK8LDm22k6KZV9vGcn6M9TBJW1z8Zck5s5gHLW95FBzn1dsnlRW2bdmgy36Ryc0W20NfTz1-VD4qW6-2lXn6Plw-PN5349r0XYwxHW4CfFNw6sTv87W614WPW1lQFBKW1JB0X18kZXv4W6Xjj0S6PzZSCW6sFKy576dYgPN6zsJJ6fYB_3W7Cm2YK6kVjg6W6PhlDC6G4Qd8W707X4P4w5mLDW5kC1x95z-4P1W4ZD0yn2JfVz8W4rqsrz9bfp_zW8pzrHd2PnmQ4V9GMVD3hmSwQW6pl-c0944ZQZf7nffJ604)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Symphony, the first **native DEX aggregator on Sei** Network, **reached $40M in cumulative volume** this week alongside major product updates and broader network momentum. On July 10, it launched the open alpha of **Composer**, a **bonding curve tool for token launches** featuring SEIYAN rebates and automatic DEX listings. Symphony has processed over **557,000 swaps from 3,400+ traders**, with **$1.1M in daily volume and $16.4M in the past 30 days**, solidifying its role in Sei’s DeFi infrastructure. The milestone coincides with [Sei’s explosive growth](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VVDzhS2DNmhfW7s0Dk_65fPQcW8kTzPf5z9JQBN54gmPK3qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3kWW6yZlGF4HBP3lW8QM5xD2vh_93N38-NCj6LY3XW8mP4qS5LcmndW2KCCKz7cbKLLN4FBd4ZYnvQVW6CSFmP58bG9BW3t9YPR68P2rXN706KFDjMWHwW2DjVs85DdL8RW2cpck42X_jLzW8n4dqH4njmdWW5dg8zW7dRDtxW2xQCYc5xB9q3N2JqjWF1WZtsW4j-VFF5PS1RRW6XSVh259HK6dW38pMw980bGYFW7kXwk_4TfnkmW2hD-Rt207g6mW86Vxgx5zy0C8W95VGhH1BrgmbW9dgKzS19C6cSW2QCs5m1BMGb2W485yh_8FpmbMW6NBj1H7fQNl3f7jrBgM04)
: TVL surpassed $680M (+224% YTD), daily active users hit an all-time high of 880K, and transactions crossed 2M on July 14.

![TOTAL WEEKLY VOLUME](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fcc46c41ec45a3fbbec7521ee1b1b9d583a9e144e-1028x668.png&w=3840&q=75)

[The State of Solana, Visualized](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VVDzhS2DNmhfW7s0Dk_65fPQcW8kTzPf5z9JQBN54gmPK3qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3njW34JSq6640SnvW1f9yB145Pz04W1_tyvf1h5DxhW8d-0y884J6xFN2bJNXnXd7gMW7VbCD235bZrcW78CMGz1645MjW9bdy6X52vf5SM-sDfSDp-19W9g7tlc49nSqyN2l8yLQB01hNW2Zy1Ky27jV_WW6gb22q6FZg3qVzCvXt2D7MjsW71wthG6kNdP1VYcclX4fjqWdW953cn84cRpX5W8TFwLd8X4v7NW2r65237s0y38V6Ybfb5BMhkKW7xy1gV4Md9LgW4RmY3L1rn_-sW4_Gkcv4bHhCcW5x30LW6nCrGBW4YqvGR6KGpBrW1XhHHH8dxL22f3qWd1v04)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Solana’s 2025 momentum is visible across token launchpads, DeFi, staking, and more, and this dashboard by The DeFi Report captures it in depth. The dashboard tracks key metrics like **$2.5M+ in daily Real Economic Value (REV)**, ~8% SOL staking yield (with over 6% from issuance), and a breakdown of **57M SOL in liquid staking** (14% of total). It features a **standout validator map** built via scatter chart, and shows **Helius leading with 13.2M active stake**. Other highlights include **$11B in stablecoin supply**, **$9B+ in TVL**, insights into **DEX and launchpad volumes**, **stablecoin velocity**, and SOL’s evolving token economics, making it one of the most comprehensive views of the Solana ecosystem to date.

![Chart (6)-1](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fb536f2a03013401b3339149c336b076df5e5b58a-1120x739.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

Nothing in this newsletter constitutes financial advice.

**Always do your own research.**

Dune Digest is all about cutting through the noise and surfacing the most relevant on-chain trends. If you have insights, dashboards, or data-driven stories that belong in the Digest, **drop your suggestions** [here](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VVDzhS2DNmhfW7s0Dk_65fPQcW8kTzPf5z9JQBN54gmNx5nXHCW5BWr2F6lZ3nPW3f5Pwx5s9BZBN6kXMQg9Y0-KW3PgXWC826q3tVJTcvw33PT_6W3p9XbW9lt_H4W7PqK-57QDX8lVQ1fyz9lw3CCW6RsvGf7Q9rKpTVfG54f4WttW5-70S689WJ-yW3v7z7f3NdgCpVcqQFz8qSXryW72gjcy3tvHVBN6hjXpdryn3RW7CsW7M4RtTP0W3ZC5w67yhv9mW5ZQmJW3mHyqBVvqFGm7cZCywW1P9p821Lt0PCVNqbxx6X2WQkW5-M2gg3fy9c0W5ls5KF3GGgV5W3Ln3Lb4xyx9DN98wm6cLnYmsW7QyLK45xTV_BW2VB1jr1BgVwGW3S-hdH3GdCfQVYt-27423MWMW4p6xLS8B7zn_W3BtCLG73vKM_W8FKfhQ4RCqhYW1MbNG83SRcXtW6sV8qT2kx6ZXW3vS1nq67Rn1Lf6fGp2804)
.

_The data must flow._

# Started From The Bottom - from nobody to unicorn in 3 years - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

StartedFromTheBottom\-fromnobodytounicornin3years
=================================================

We started from the bottom in the fringe industry of crypto as two first-time founders. Now we’re here: a unicorn company at only 16 employees in 3 years. Here’s the story.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Research•March 30, 2022•10 min read

![@Fredrik Haga](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F52880ee8ffdf5c96f23bc355690443daba96f1f2-664x664.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Fredrik Haga](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fredrikhaga/)
Co-Founder & CEO at Dune

![Started From The Bottom - from nobody to unicorn in 3 years](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fc2efabb4b595c21944367bf5e6c88c23b52717d8-2000x1500.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

We recently announced that we’ve raised a $69 420 000 Series B at a unicorn valuation. We started from the bottom in the fringe industry of crypto as two first-time founders when we created Dune out of Oslo, Norway in 2018. Now we’re here: a unicorn company at only 16 employees merely 3 years later. Here’s the story of our fundraising journey.

Pre-seed
--------

**Raised March 2019**

**Round size: $250 000**

**Fundraising time: 7 months**

**Team size: 2 founders**

This was by far the hardest round for us to raise. We started Dune with less than a handful of connections in the crypto space. We knew from the get-go that we would not find any potential customers nor investors understanding what we were up to in Norway.

We initially focused on cleaning up Ethereum data and getting our first customer before spending time fundraising. Only two weeks into Dune’s existence we flew to ETH Berlin and started pitching our idea of product metric dashboards; “Mixpanel for crypto”. We ended up signing Dharma as our first customer only 3 months later. 1st of December we shipped them cleaned up Ethereum data and they paid us $600/month for it. That was a huge win as hardly anyone had a paying customer in the nascent crypto space at the time.

_The photo up top is Mats and me celebrating getting our first customer with some IPA and chess._

Full of hope with our paying customer and not having had a paycheck for a few months, I started focusing on fundraising late in the autumn and assumed we’d be able to pull together a pre-seed round by the end of the year. Absolutely not. Fundraising was brutal. At the time traditional venture funds could not care less about crypto and there were probably less than ten serious crypto funds. By Christmas we’d hustled our way to around 50 conversations, but we were not even close to getting a term sheet.

The main pushback was that the addressable market for crypto tooling was ridiculously small and that there was not any business to be made in crypto data because it was all open. Going into Christmas 2018 it was becoming clear that we’d probably not be funded anytime soon. Being broke, having no salary and getting nos or non-replies 10 times a day is hard.

Over Christmas we thought long and hard about the pushback we got. Part of our thesis for starting Dune was that Ethereum and crypto represent a new and more meaningful financial infrastructure that’s perfect for builders. We deeply believed that and basically disregarded all market size concerns from investors - pivoting to selling trading data to suits was not an option.

On the openness of the data however, we did see the critique of selling access to dashboards B2B maybe not being too powerful with the underlying data being open. We had two paying customers at this point, and that was our only traction. We decided to put those deals at risk and made the hard decision to pivot to a free and open community product.

> “
> 
> We realized that the powerful play here would be to embrace and accelerate the openness of the data, instead of selling a closed dashboarding product resembling web 2 data products.

With our new direction fundraising was still far from a home run. We spent another two months getting no’s and non-replies, and probably had another 50+ conversations that went nowhere. Luckily, when we were standing at the very edge and close to having to get another job, Teck at Binance was impressed by us actually having revenue and accepted us into their accelerator program.

![ width=](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fff2840c94cd69cc8297aa3796492f532fe169e8c-2000x1500.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

_ETH Denver 2019, a couple of weeks from having to give up this Dune thing due to lack of funding._

After the acceptance, I remember getting on the phone with Mats and simply laying down on the floor in joy and relief that we’d finally secured Dune’s continued existence. The following $4 000 paycheck was the best payroll of my life, after 7 months of no salary.

I think there is an interesting lesson to be learned from this: both disregard _and_ embrace VC pushback. Keep your conviction and don’t get discouraged from a 100 investor no’s, _but_ also take pushback seriously, and rigorously assess your beliefs. We essentially disregarded all market size concerns and stuck to our first principles conviction that crypto is a more meaningful financial infrastructure where there will be plenty of builders to serve. On the other hand we also took pushback to heart and adjusted our plans accordingly, embracing the openness of crypto data instead of working against it by selling closed dashboards.

Seed
----

**Raised August 2020**

**Round size: $2 000 000**

**Fundraising time: 15 months**

**Team size: 2 founders**

At the end of the Binance Accelerator in spring 2019 there was a pitch day in SF where everyone tried to raise a seed round. If I remember correctly, one or two out of the 10 companies in the batch managed to raise a seed round. We certainly did not.

We went back home to Oslo, kept our burn rate minimal, and didn't hire anyone. Mats and I had an annual salary of $50 000 and kept at it. Building the product, backfilling missing data, talking to users, tweeting out dashboards, going to hackathons, sharing a room in shitty hotels (and I’m a heavy snorer - sorry, Mats!), doing support, got on calls with users to help them learn SQL, and: trying to do a seed round.

For the next year we did four serious attempts at raising a seed round, with no luck. We had $30k ARR and a growing user base including many top projects in the space. Nevertheless, most investors still didn’t believe in the crypto opportunity, and especially not the data part of it. We went to SF in February 2020 and simply could not raise a $1M seed round. In hindsight it is pretty absurd that someone could have gotten ~10% of Dune for $1M less than two years ago, but not a single investor in Silicon Valley was interested.

![ width=](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F03709a0e07d72003175feb796b6cc03acdb95c42-1991x2000.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

_Mats and me pitching Dune in Palo Alto February 2020 trying to raise $1M. Went home with zero term sheets._

Dune was born and raised in a bear market. Finally, in summer 2020, that bear market was coming to an end. It was “DeFi Summer” and Dune was a key destination for keeping track of all the humble yield farming, staking rewards and YAM insanity. We did $60k ARR and were growing our key metrics about 5% weekly.

On our fifth attempt at a seed round 15 months after the accelerator pitch day we finally got somewhere. Getting the round together was still not easy, and we had to apply some serious hustle. No one wanted to lead the round so we ended up pricing it ourselves. It was certainly a stressful couple of weeks. We weren’t sure if it would happen this time either, but eventually we managed to get the raise done. We were finally ready to take Dune to the next level with what felt like a shit-ton of money: $2M.

Series A
--------

**Raised May 2021**

**Round size: $8 000 000**

**Team size: 6**

**Fundraising time: 1 week**

At the end of 2020 the v1 of our app, a fork of the open source analytics tool Redash, was falling apart as we had scaled to a few thousand users. Redash was a great MVP for us but the query execution queue was in the thousands, the app was slow and clunky, we had no proper public user profiles and so on and so forth. Something needed to be done. After over 2 years as a two man army, with no and low salaries, we finally had the money to hire a few more teammates. In Q1 2021 the four-person Dune-team with two founders and our first hires Wilhelm and Vegard (+ a consultant) took on an insanely ambitious project: rebuilding the Dune application from the ground up.

We had no idea how long it would take us, but it was undoubtedly a massive endeavor. Thousands of queries, user flows and visualizations needed to be supported. After an intense effort we shipped Dune v2 on the 18th of March - we delivered faster and better than we could have dared to hope for. A small and dedicated team can take something from 0 to 100, real quick.

By late April we were feeling good, crypto had continued to grow, and we had hired a couple of more team members bringing our total team size to 6 people. For a couple of months we had also started seeing something we were not used to: investors pinging us. They had been seeing Dune charts across crypto-Twitter and wanted to chat. While we felt like the seed round was just done, we realized there was a real opportunity at hand here.

In late April we said fuck it and decided to go for a Series A raise. Mats and I made the decision on a Friday. I turned around and asked the VCs I knew if they would have a chat the week after. Over the weekend I put together a deck and started circulating before the calls. I remember saying to my girlfriend “I’m not sure I can do that long weekend ski trip in a month, as I will probably be fundraising then.”

However, things had changed, we were suddenly hot. On the following Tuesday we got two term sheets: one at valuation we considered decent, and then one at twice the valuation - it was bonkers! From there things escalated like crazy. Partners that said they could only speak to us in a month could speak on 30 minutes notice. The FOMO was on and Mats and I pitched partners baaaack to baack for 12-14 hours a day through Wednesday and Thursday. By the end of the week we had a handful of term sheets and could probably had a fair amount more.

‍

![ width=](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F3298d4b2771ccc3c27c7b1bbd1f538c70b1a672e-2000x1500.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

_A walk in the park deciding on term sheets_

10PM Friday, one week after we decided to raise, we signed a term sheet with our dream partner USV for a $8M round. It was the most intense and exhausting week of my life and we were totally overwhelmed by suddenly being “hot” to VCs, but it was certainly better than the 2 years of no’s and no-replies. Tables turn, live and learn.

Series B
--------

**Raised November 2021**

**Round size: $69 420 000**

**Team size: 16**

**Fundraising time: 0 days**

Fast forward 6 months after the Series A. Crypto kept getting hotter and more mainstream, NFTs blew up and you could track OpenSea volumes on Dune among a ton of other things. We kept growing our key metric 30% month/month. From August to September it grew a mindboggeling 175% month/month - all organic with zero marketing spend. We had grown the team to 16 people.

Now in stark contrast from two years earlier all tech investors wanted their crypto play and they were certainly seeing Dune charts everywhere. After the Series A experience we were not too wooed by investors wanting to chat anymore and I turned down a handful of investor requests daily. At the end of the day it’s all about building product, community and team, so that’s what we kept doing.

However, some time in October, Coatue managed to slip through the tight “no VCs filter” and I had a 30 minute chat with them. They were clearly impressed with Dune, displayed a deep understanding of what we’re up to and wanted us to let them know if we were fundraising. I told them we are not fundraising.

We didn’t chat for a few weeks, and then they pinged me and said they had undertaken a due diligence of us outside-in. That was of course interesting and we got on a quick call where they showed me a 40 slides deck with a ton of customer interviews and insights about our market opportunity. It was all looking good and they said just let us know if you are fundraising. Again, I said “cool, but we’re not fundraising”.

A couple of weeks later I got pinged again and they wanted to do a 10 minute call. At this point I was experienced enough to understand what that meant: a preemptive term sheet. I took the call and sure enough: they wanted to invest $50M at $500M - a very solid markup from our Series A half a year earlier. They also showed me some really really impressive data that they track, which gave them strong conviction on what we’re up to from the outside-in.

In this call I also realized they were based on the West Coast, not New York as I thought. They were taking calls at 5AM to meet my suggested European time slots. Knowing their location I said I’d be happy to take the calls later in the evening, but they simply replied “we’ll work your time zone, no worries”.

We were however still very much not fundraising, so I told them they would basically have to give us an offer we simply couldn’t refuse, and said “make it a billion and we’ll see”. They told me they would have to circle up internally, but that it didn’t sound entirely undoable.

And now you know where this is going: two days later they come back with a term sheet at, _boom_, $1B. An offer we couldn’t refuse. And with that we had landed a unicorn round. What a time to be alive. Oh, and for the memes we made the total round size $69 420 000, yolo.

![ width=](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fda395a59e5971ac88f0f73bca2030695e8798ce8-2000x1500.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

_Ah shit, here we go again. Over some good coffee we decided to say fuck it, and go for the Series B._

Getting funding for building Dune has been one hell of a ride with a lot of serious struggles and some amazing moments of joy. Interestingly, funding size and time spent fundraising has basically inversely correlated. If you’re building a startup remember that things can change quickly, you can grind for a long long time, with little or no progress, but when you hit it things can go absurdly fast. Just over 3 years ago we started from the bottom - now we’re here.

Back to building til it's over, but it's far from over... so [we are hiring](https://dune.com/careers)
!

**Bonus**

If these tracks have been playing in your head while reading this: bingo! Congrats on winning the Drizzy pop quizzy.

# Acaduneia: How Academics are Using Dune - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

Acaduneia:HowAcademicsareUsingDune
==================================

Let’s take a look at what academia has been writing, examine their conclusions, the Dune data they use, and think about how we can expand on their work as a community.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Research•November 29, 2022•6 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

![Acaduneia: How Academics are Using Dune](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F0252738c0040d6f9a2a79cd312188fdea576be4d-1200x675.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

Dune Wizards are a diverse group. They come from all backgrounds, geographies, and skill levels - and do their magic everywhere from spare bedrooms to the halls of institutional power.

Some are first-time analysts, taking their first steps into data through Dune. Others have vast data science experience and use Dune as their go-to tool for checking the chain.

Others are some of the top academic & research institutions in the world!

Over the past few months, we’ve seen several interesting papers come out of the academic and think tank sphere, from well-known institutions like:

1.  Stanford
2.  Cornell
3.  UPenn
4.  LBS
5.  The IMF
6.  Bank of Canada
7.  Scalability and fragmentation in crypto payment systems.
8.  NFT product/market fit and pricing theories.
9.  Crypto standards development.
10.  Slippage reduction in AMM trading.
11.  The (inevitable?) centralization of governance tokens.
12.  Frederic Boissay, Giulio Cornelli, Sebastian Doerr, and Jon Frost
13.  ‍[Blockchain scalability and the fragmentation of crypto](https://www.bis.org/publ/bisbull56.pdf)
    
14.  ‍[Bank for International Settlements Bulletin No 56](https://www.bis.org/)
    
15.  Blockchains rely on native token prices to ensure security.
16.  High token prices drive new entrants to alternative chains, leading to fragmentation.
17.  Thus, as a blockchain grows it experiences anti-network effects (more users = pressure to shrink the network)
18.  Money is a social coordination tool, and traditional payment methods and networks grow through network effects - thus “there is more promise in innovations that build on trust in sovereign currencies.”
19.  How can we better understand users' flow from one chain to another based on chain usage and native token price?
20.  What metrics do we have and need to measure the monetary qualities of crypto assets?
21.  What does the industry need to have better interoperability? What data can we surface to support those efforts?
22.  [Roman Kräussl](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/cf_dev/AbsByAuth.cfm?per_id=355071)
    , Universite du Luxembourg - Department of Finance; Hoover Institution, Stanford University
23.  [Alessandro Tugnetti](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/cf_dev/AbsByAuth.cfm?per_id=5249406)
    , University of Luxembourg - Department of Finance
24.  **‍**[Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs): A Review of Pricing Determinants, Applications and Opportunities](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4112429)
    
25.  **‍**[SSRN](https://www.ssrn.com/index.cfm/en/)
    
26.  NFTs share the investment profile of physical collectibles, namely a high yield with a high-risk profile.
27.  NFT prices move with BTC and ETH, in part because many people move their crypto wealth into NFTs - to speculate on an investment, to express their passion for a particular creator or collection, and to become members of the virtual communities associated with certain NFTs.
28.  Because NFT markets are even newer than cryptocurrencies, they’re even earlier on the price discovery curve and thus very inefficient markets; we don’t have a definitive NFT pricing model as we do for fine art.
29.  The oracle problem: there have been relatively few attempts to link NFT pricing to real-world data like real-time currency exchange rates to better price NFTs, or to check the delivery status of a physical product that comes with an NFT purchase to enable an extra level of buyer protection.
30.  The interconnection problem: though token bridges have somewhat solved the cross-chain transfer challenge for fungible tokens, the lack of cross-chain transferability for NFTs means the markets on different chains have to, at least in part, be considered separately when thinking about pricing.
31.  What data and metrics can we use to understand and define the key properties of each main category of NFTs?
32.  How about the pricing models discussed? What dashboards can be created to compare different collections to themselves or others through the lenses of hedonic regression models, repeat sales regressions, vector autoregressive models, machine learning, and wavelet models?
33.  How can we combine Dune blockchain data with other sources to tell a complete picture (e.g. the Google Trends to Unique NFT Wallets graph)?
34.  [Sarah Hammer](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/cf_dev/AbsByAuth.cfm?per_id=2615231)
    , The Wharton School of the University of Pennsylvania
35.  [Brett Hemenway Falk](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/cf_dev/AbsByAuth.cfm?per_id=3269274)
    , University of Pennsylvania - Department of Computer and Information Science
36.  **‍**[Taming the Wild West: Achieving Public Policy Goals through Crypto Standards](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4096816)
    
37.  ‍[SSRN](https://www.ssrn.com/)
    
38.  They can reduce development costs by allowing multiple organizations to reuse (standardized) smart contract code.
39.  They enable interoperability (see the 60k+ tokens that can be swapped on Uniswap thanks to ERC-20)
40.  They can be made global, similar to the Unicode character encoding standards, to facilitate international trade and cooperation.
41.  Since they’re voluntary, industry-wide input is needed for their development and formalization so the industry itself has a vested interest in compliance.
42.  Since they don’t have the force of law, the potential repercussions of parties not adhering to standards have to be considered.
43.  Standards implemented on-chain cannot verify the content of the data put on-chain via oracles - so issues around accuracy and fraud will still exist.
44.  On-chain standards can’t control off-chain activities, so compliance and enforcement will have to be handled somehow.
45.  How widely used are OpenZeppelin’s “reference implementations?” Can their adoption rate be analyzed to examine the potential opportunities and challenges in creating blockchain standards?
46.  More and more centralized exchanges are putting proof of reserves on-chain - what metrics need to be monitored to contextualize this information and provide early warnings if a CEX may be in trouble?
47.  What are the key oracles relied on by the crypto industry? How can we see the extent to which certain oracles are referenced?
48.  **‍**[Irene Aldridge](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/cf_dev/AbsByAuth.cfm?per_id=1210072)
    , Cornell University
49.  **‍**[Slippage in AMM Markets](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4133897)
    
50.  **‍**[SSRN](https://www.ssrn.com/)
    
51.  _Positivity in the quantities of both instruments to ensure that positive quantities are traded._
52.  _Convexity, to ensure that the prices rise when liquidity falls._
53.  _Asymptotic properties with respect to both x and y axes to ensure support for potentially infinite liquidity._
54.  A review of the key differences between Automated Market Making (AMM), which is the most popular format for Decentralized Exchanges, and traditional limit-order book-based market making.
55.  A step-by-step analysis of crypto-token price formation.
56.  A proposal of one solution for ex-ante slippage estimation that can be used to better trade on AMMs.
57.  Can you identify trades with positive and negative slippage and compare by tokens traded, DEXs, and time?
58.  How do trading volumes relate to Liquidity Pool sizes? Do some pools have much more liquidity than they need to minimize slippage given their average volume?
59.  What can we infer about Orderbook vs AMM trade models based on the usage of each in different protocols?
60.  [Joaquin Delgado Fernandez](https://orbilu.uni.lu/browse?type=author&value=Delgado+Fernandez%2C+Joaquin+50040094)
    , University of Luxembourg
61.  [Tom Josua Barbereau](https://orbilu.uni.lu/browse?type=author&value=Barbereau%2C+Tom+Josua+50042838)
    , University of Luxembourg
62.  [Orestis Papageorgiou](https://orbilu.uni.lu/browse?type=author&value=Papageorgiou%2C+Orestis+50043384)
    , University of Luxembourg
63.  **‍**[Agent-based Model of Initial Token Allocations](https://orbilu.uni.lu/handle/10993/51934)
    
64.  **‍**[University of Luxembourg](https://orbilu.uni.lu/)
    
65.  Does trading behavior affect voting rights token distributions over time?
66.  Do alternative, ’fair launch’ token allocations affect voting rights token distributions over time?
67.  An agent-based model for the analysis of token distributions under various market conditions reflective of trading.
68.  Simulation results showing how over time, regardless of initial token allocation, concentration is imminent.
69.  Extended understandings of tokenomics to formerly include token allocations as part of governance parameters.

Though they conclude the fair launch allocation pioneered with $YFI did not effectively combat governance token concentration, they acknowledge a few limitations:

1.  Their clearing mechanism lacks a formal price clearing method, and they didn’t implement limit orders.
2.  In their model, the decision-making of an individual agent does not depend on past decisions or those of other agents.
3.  They rely heavily on the [Fear Greed Index](https://alternative.me/crypto/fear-and-greed-index/)
     as a proxy for market conditions instead of more granular indicators like specific asset prices or social media data.
4.  How do the different distribution methods outlined here compare to each other in the real world? (Schedule-based; Pre-mined, scheduled distribution; Pre-mined, one-off distribution; Discretionary)
5.  What about the allocation or incentive systems? (See Table 7.)
6.  How do Voting Escrow (“ve”) tokens like veCRV, which are less liquid tokens used for voting, compare to non-escrowed, highly liquid governance tokens?

The research must flow
----------------------

What an interesting research area! This is just a _small sample_ of more than 20 research papers that have come across the Dune desk in recent months. There's so much interesting work being done, and so much more for all of us to explore too.

The great thing about blockchain data though, is that you don’t need to be a PHd candidate at a prestigious institution to access and crunch it with professional tools. All you need is some SQL chops and a Dune account.

Anon Wizards can put out analysis just as good as Ivy League Professors!

It’s great to see these institutions really diving deep into the frontiers of finance, and we’re looking forward to more papers.

Make no mistake about it - what’s happening here on the frontiers of finance is very interesting to those who walk the halls of higher institutions - but learning, understanding and analyzing it isn’t limited to those halls anymore........

# Dune Digest 016 - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

DuneDigest016
=============

Polymarket & Kalshi, Kaia USDT, EulerSwap, Hyperliquid, Uniswap v4 hooks
------------------------------------------------------------------------

News•June 28, 2025•3 min read

[Kaiakaia](https://dune.com/blockchains/kaia)

![@Filippo Armani](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Ff9dd54cabfaa63ae0dda792b5072e44d5b9b9971-800x800.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Filippo Armani](https://www.linkedin.com/in/filarm/)
Data Content Creator at Dune

![Dune Digest 016](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fe0eb9e3c389d75f85cd686081cdce17121775a2b-1200x675.png&w=3840&q=75)

[Polymarket and Kalshi, the InfoFi Unicorns](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5HY3qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3lNW37j2Z07YR3kRVcRx1-8s8j-mW3_bbmb3BcqzGW3jhgPK15hZmFW8vNXS_3tG4xTW8TkD3P6TGkKQW5MLh9K50hnYkW7_5xT75qSFnxW7TW6wq1mpfDWW3ByJD83JpqHtW2SPFQq8GPtc4W37ck2L1rn9XNW619sK26jQGS4W1CBp6h7TytHCW8Bnwp54SvjJsW12LsJd78P_zHW1KxgdV6XgS84W62dBSZ66PZz2W3Qpw273xZqfFW6TmZq73XLB79VcZ4Wj3RyWmnMc5gmwZ9qmmW1XQjjn29pLjBW8QmP9q3NWbXgW1TSpj-4hhJV5N4Nx0Lq1DfvXW6PScjv3b41CgW9knP1C5JDnXxf2rz3Zl04)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Two of the biggest players in the prediction markets space made headlines almost simultaneously this past week: **Polymarket is** [reportedly](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5G85nXHCW50kH_H6lZ3l8VSdK8X2hVQm5W7yySdN1n542qN8xWBLz2gTNTW6K5Kp03hMDBmW5w4dCn8r8rwTW3ZTD8q5GVFh1W8j49K16Z0SCWW8qNcyN2F1fJFW1zHhVl4V2V9QN9jCs4F-NxB4W1frs968gY38BW5VYvcP5GJZ8GW622B1z5TPbsrW89Qhtp6YX8vqVHdnh37_6NMCW3xxD-R4M8CxhW8t901q1Cth1mW7MHdVY63X0b9N28f15D1sjsxW6S7_SG61v-KvW1JMNVM1Mc5wXW3CHZMM2NftYNW4rtBcd1_v0NDN2bYkDBvNCNCW6hJLnq32cwH_W1_gMy27mpHBnW8hX9N74-0-StW3-tJPm41X0_lW4TnLrg6lKrxhW5J-b_t4qgjGbW42mgyq6jvQ12W6Sy5x828rS6vf1_sTKn04)
 **raising $200M at a $1B** valuation led by Founders Fund, while **Kalshi** [closed](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5Gs5nXHCW5BWr2F6lZ3kQW2r25-D6zLtgcVR8Mp58wcTkhW5swphL2b7Z3PW2bHH2f8dcgm1W6BKMNK5vHLlgW6LrM328THMwJN3y6mYfhJBWCW13jL2r2MdHr0N7Fks0WvXykHW6wwzTC4YhG-fW75GgYX15kgYmVGpLz01G2rK6W82s5zj9fcMtsW7Z50bL8hfT9gN1qQzlJsHnH7W51ljW45k3BVBW9kyw9z2db3wPW1Xv07F1hPZRpW351jt97qzW_jVTYK_75SYvXKW9hqtvB80YZbWVPXfQy2Vzt5XV4980c61QSrsW75-MZL18RxtPW2wz4vj1bNl6-W5g0Vgp6ZtDXBW9f69mX3RSn9YW6MHtqy1nsk9rVdzfns5xxF1SW5_J7yw6Wkw9TVC5FQQ1Rr8LcVY661w53jQyKW5Gvd102TrR1_W8mLGtj3RRSFCf85-Dx404)
 **a Paradigm-led $185M round** at **$2B valuation**. Despite regulatory hurdles that limit its accessibility, Polymarket continues to show strong demand. After dipping from a $2.6B peak in November 2024, **volume** has been steadily rising and **surpassed $1B in June 2025**. Adding to the momentum, X [announced](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5G85nXHCW50kH_H6lZ3mSW4Yt2ny1X-xZ3N651ctgxtrK8W462xsz8-ZFhCW69CHfH4YjB0-W6tRx7F6j5hR3W6xzclP3VLc3nW1njP9z350yj4W3K4tp01QC2HJW1Hrsw34qFkWNW6wYCnz7PS4h0W1Y9cz864hxFrVpnZRV3jswKTW8byPqh5bTRClW7zK3P38f4C3sW6JbBMG3FH8ZBW4zgkXC8FQX9DW1M2ZLS6YJlN_N1b7rfx9-5r6W48jvDw6lmvjgW1ts2j16kRz_xW2Fg69t2Q4p4lW6DfcdS52cVd6N1Y1YsLBqV3tW3dj1kT8TzrQ0W4m_2B52DLw6RW3z9lWQ8hBKqhW6lWMsl4c05jNW3-x6rs70fJMsW9g-VFK5NB1kKVkNpdg8DprkNMC4t7-vK7Y-W8hD3fB5FYllrf5nZ_6C04)
 a partnership with Polymarket to integrate real-time prediction data, Grok’s AI analysis, and X posts into a shared product delivering live, data-driven insights. Prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket are shaping up to be early pillars of InfoFi, where information is not just shared but valued, traded, and monetized directly.

![Volume USD per month](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F8a0ad9e34f4c5077dbcc01d63584e69a2d42d4e3-1120x659.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

[Kaia Brings Native USDT to 196M LINE Users](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5HF3qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3pjW90qWYb3gspvNW3LzwND2QvrJcW4My9Gb5sm9VjW76S8TM4ng5LtN7Mt8rjPXkzRV67HCM8cdPy-W3yZPfR8nZypYW5SDgJN2WV8M2W4wyKjX3Ph6kmW6Qg5KJ54Tqb1MZVK3hDdkcCN4gdjzM2PKTXW8ZkyQj2L12GsW8N7pL07Pg4xZVVf2RN69jXGyN5fCWrt1KZKQW1MB-T13m_vCHW61ggcm3jGQnqN32mf9gBPbSRW4mzrn05yx2PRW2vb5Zw5jvLrvW3D9Gs07wVX8lN8GxQJSwbGZjW42r5L_7NyT1wW4g-mWV2gtyR9W5gtpP83S1KVNf5bHRZT04)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In May, Kaia launched native support for USDT, and this week, Hashed Official just published the first onchain data showing **$100M in USDT supply**. While user activity has normalized from April’s record 5.5 million **daily active accounts to around 500K**, the addition of native USDT marks a key step in expanding Kaia’s utility and enabling more stablecoin-based applications across its ecosystem. Through LINE NEXT’s Mini Dapp platform and self-custodial wallet, users can now send, receive, and use USDT for payments, rewards, and DeFi. As Kaia positions itself as a gateway for dollar-backed stablecoins in Asia, the launch of native USDT marks a major step in bringing stable, accessible Web3 experiences to mainstream consumers.

![Stablecoins on Kaia](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F5d529bf8fa8be71f32658a7bd54339c883cd19dc-1120x684.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

[EulerSwap Hits $300M+ in Beta](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5HF3qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3lzW8W8v7m8xp75zW61VVnQ2N2bPDW5YQvnl7tMZn5N8MRqNF_-5XJW8gq-9J2ChLM0W3HGGZp3qRmXLW7XQHyC2fzmqPW5X2C1D8hW0_mMN2H7rdhBGLW7l4H8B5Th8PRN6vvc3Py_DdLW8DpYtN1cXlkvW3n6Jc12fHjNWW95fvcF5tNjSPW3c1Fjv2FtY1fW8ZM1-M55CGwzW5BYhsk4dq5XnW1dSnMK5frl_GW90fpsQ6Jq9mDW4Cvv9T9cfb3nW8jJqFM78w7-YW1qThbx34vYk_W3MvWbH56KtPvW6tp84B8s70ljN25HQt8WKqgwW998DX17X6477f1RZykT04)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Euler, the DeFi lending protocol, has launched its own DEX, EulerSwap, and it's already **crossed $300M in cumulative trading volume** while still in beta. Built on top of **Uniswap v4’s hook architecture** and deeply integrated with Euler’s lending vaults, the DEX allows LPs to earn swap fees, lending yield, and borrow against positions, all with the same capital. This unified design unlocks new levels of capital efficiency for DeFi traders and liquidity providers. While EulerSwap pushes the protocol’s reach wider, Euler is also going deeper, [expanding](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5HY3qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3myW8MjzNy6Yr9rVW38qq1K1RnnFFN27w0z7WDcxSW6-G6Xz7Q4mxrW3Wk54n2DwJ0FVlbYdD4GJ2GBW64D_KJ58xqTJW1jn9zd3qyJLGW49n_Nn1cqN_FW3Z42jy2ccSNgW1mKy9s7fbz6-W8Xg7Yg7ZBJX6W4jvB1D8VgnZfW6dgZKs8mP5KyW1vqy8L6LJPmxW5bttmr55F_4wW5396443n-trYW4jfs-D7WWGLzW5F_Q4l8dGlfNW88s-276j85kKW2gJQnQ24xX9_W1nsCQK9d8xY3VVCCpm6TVFN8W8wSnBl5kjB7KW7t6h5N3PQr83W7MDVX06VRX7LW96w7NY58BpxRW7fTht_5s84Qjf7g8_K804)
 its core lending product to Arbitrum this week.

![Trading Volume (1)](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F4c0397cca3f608d92a6cff64e64e31a11d0686c6-1036x682.png&w=3840&q=75)

[Hyperliquid Tops $1.5T Volume and $300M Revenue](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5HF3qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3lyW6Yc-ZP84gw29VQ4j5X8hFy9xW1BpqnK4F1M1SW4RYX8836lyL8W7hM5J36R0WjNW1DxdCP6hT5jxW37jbfG9bNPqZW4vkfc23MBk02W5x12W-2cSGnYV1WqPq52j9xzW9b1KwN4CTvZmW5fF0M89kykWXW5b-F4q9hBgmKW6jLlVT1G5-rjV5GhFS4BttlMW2z1K9s6ZzJlRW2Sm7TG63C7d0W3VSCcp4gdk-zW59nDkS27_8MnW8t87bD3T2c3rW4kNYc31qr_CTW3tXKrl1KsPQKVJq5G12-nZVTW8nNgk34v9B_dW4VF9v81C07kmN57L5N0zJysJf5HSV_z04)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The new Hyperliquid’s dashboard by Sealaunch reveals the scale of its performance: **$1.57 trillion in annual perp trading volume**, **$309 million in cumulative revenue**, and over **$3.5 billion in bridge TVL**. Monthly revenue remains strong, with **$55 million generated in June alone**. Built from first principles with custom infrastructure, including the HyperBFT consensus and HyperCore execution engine, Hyperliquid offers fully onchain perps with one-block finality and support for 200K orders per second. As the protocol scales and deepens its market share in onchain derivatives, **developers now have access to HyperEVM data via Dune’s** [Sim API](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5HY3qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3nLVMTSv047H58qW3fflRp940pB0W41VKYY2G-4hNN8PVDPlJWRzYW1SyCQ310wKGqW230Wjz2DX50CW6NjJDx2tNqG3W7H0PJW3PQKkrW7fscP539GxyWW6D_cSd6r6bkHW6qNbq28YlYbMN9fxk3NtgL0fW5vN2tW2PsVMVW2kHB4R7hF_H3W3NJb3141YMZYW2SxRSC1STb2gVVB1S16my0b2W3z3KyC5XHPtcN8L3bXd72wbhW39jPT618j3xBW6xjrpS3yYGg2N12ZHzlTt8xtW3P9NkN1ww4g3W7MFd9-3l2lCNW2G2cZV6lDN6KW2TLrKZ9bGzpWW134BZl3zFmlqW9h7Rc33y07vrf6nkqb-04)
, unlocking deeper integrations and use cases.

![HyperLiquid Revenue (1)](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fe41440ea24ad8f2a0793a747c7e15d04a2d27937-1120x702.png&w=3840&q=75)

[Uniswap v4 Hooks Top $380M Volume](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5HF3qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3mjW2nvHs53pbty2W2gMVGv5gSpCNN3tKNj-CCKN3W89hh8j8WtMNdVzPqZH2bk1yGW8Q_ZMF8jwRk7W55_V_W48R_vyW7gCN-x3Z3hRdVTJYHb5SGMP3N41dHFKP1VX-VpG0Vm7FZXN5N6RrytX90ZxqMm3-j6Mlh95W6D3s8k6wkcPtW2HQHS47dVJXhVGzxvQ50xr7MW30q9yP64sfbXW2KtX1q69MtFCW8HBjX635fQvyW40GJnk1_-HjwVWWDV_6QQKJ9W3241wP7qGyT9W6W7sPz3ZzN8nW4-MJNq71y0bQW1WYqch621b8rW3GwqlH7MWQfsf8Lx_yz04)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Roughly two weeks after Uniswap announced its first [Hook Design Lab](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5G85nXHCW50kH_H6lZ3ndW5RWfXm1drxNVW5-t8bn8cSHJrW634-C-8kFfb8W1wyBZJ8tpVSHW7vg0sK3VcfpMW3D6YsM2F_68RW64Y5y15YBnrZN8G5jqYMFG3MN7W5CcGrQpvQVzxPqv7vfYPkW4s52jz3D4m2CW3yPYzt2x4KjpW8zY_VZ7wydszW51bRYN8RLsksN4CjNmpjQQZcMFKKGb7j11YW1J8RHs7Zg_ksW2MZHgm1BrV7RW7WZK8v1xrw4RW6v6nVd8PMvCDW2zxtgf7Q6PJkW3c5pzg4jX_cQN5t7cP-P3FZSW5spV855VnjMFW7LCw612G74cfW2FhTGY27NScMVyfq7C8l3KlVW8c9Vh85lPgnCW1lmwJM32-cpGW6pmfbs8qzNmKN299YJQgkkzVW4pGTsX8VktXyf5DV1QY04)
 cohort, hook-enabled pools are quietly gaining momentum. **Total hooked pool volume has surpassed $382M**, with **June averaging around $8M per day,** driven largely by activity on **Unichain**. Among the **top-performing hooks are EulerSwap** ($325M total volume), **Bunni** ($388M), and **Aegis** ($70M), all part of Cohort 1. Others like Kyber, Zora, and Gamma Strategies are also seeing growing volumes without incentives. The hooked share of Uniswap v4 volume is now approaching 4%, signaling growing traction for this modular new paradigm in AMM design.

![Chart-2](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F16a68f8b0cb8e6bbe90c92e7e214a57738bf5907-1120x701.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

Nothing in this newsletter constitutes financial advice.

**Always do your own research.**

Dune Digest is all about cutting through the noise and surfacing the most relevant on-chain trends. If you have insights, dashboards, or data-driven stories that belong in the Digest, **drop your suggestions** [here](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWGBMv7ZlNNyW4rXV6F2x_tpWW1HN78y5ylJwhN9cd5Gs5nXHCW5BWr2F6lZ3lcW3hkM-w3vl5LcVYLX1P2phNPQW4CgPY23nYqYQVCtx703KBY4gW2ZHngm2pt2s5W84JQ6t4qGt7hW50DKtg797T7nW62sjxB5f7Dj4W85RKGj8Fj3X9W4pSxrZ7gVF9zW6vMr9F2-8lxVW2y23rC64VQ2cW1Gj81y3vwZzkW3Nn-m31W1G8VW79pHHN1c9rdrW68K5v34QBzlDW3TlKL46Khv3SW6LCZr-68QQXgW1tF4GT5qNmb4W3-cGBW4TVq2mW9f19Bq5cWrYwW11fp985hZtBsW2156Ck3_vVTTW14xZF03H_BjvW4K6M9P8MtWpdW2rlCyr6FC886W6fksHP6r-9pRW8sGKWT20fslNW93H3ZL4b0L-vVBc4m51frTnZW5K-17z13sxy_W1NWpLF7HgrLQW8-Kmjj3tWntHW4xt_l_4kxnK5f4S-N8x04)
.

# Dune Digest 030 - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

DuneDigest030
=============

RWA's special edition: BENJI, thBILL, DRIP, Ondo GM, Centrifuge SPXA, Aave Horizon, nTBILL, nCREDIT
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

News•October 6, 2025•2 min read

![@Filippo Armani](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Ff9dd54cabfaa63ae0dda792b5072e44d5b9b9971-800x800.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Filippo Armani](https://www.linkedin.com/in/filarm/)
Data Content Creator at Dune

![Dune Digest 030](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2Fd295a8b308e6c28f3bba2424802d0beca25bcd67-1200x675.webp&w=3840&q=75)

Since we shipped the [RWA Report 2025](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6sqP3qn9qW7lCdLW6lZ3nSW20w6VK5d_NZQW5FNRB-2HpfHqW4KlDf-3WVB_6W2ZXqTd7SrD_vN4LH8Ky13J7TN7WhLfbqt470N3nSdxLfKv4vW8Jzc101VZyG4W7nMxWn2ptVdQW3rXqbd32cWW7W4N12YH8fb14zW47hNGX4MBvXDW2tf0xQ15lTmhW3dkmp85vbH19N8xw1qvDZ3QHW3g1N1D34rqF4W6zVtW64Mtvx9W5lhR8b2f6S1_W8fq4gQ8dQLqSW961DbR7tr83ZMDGphB6JjRZW1g3xQd50J2dmW2jy6H311N_MKW7t6PBX23kCbyf77ZBVF04)
, the market has kept sprinting and September has seen —validating our four theses in real time.

[Treasuries as the base layer](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6sr43qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3lDW4QFxRF2Sv3myW1kzxVB27PkRLV_9VXp7N69LrW3Yqky14FQv6bW455pxn80Yv1cN5t0pKY59J6-W5v-KlW4g2dLbN51xrYkNqhMQW5c0Ccq4yVpDtW8b7yHc464hLcVjVVDh36n-csVyxQyS3hylDRW8dcpYf77kyKzW6Zh5y47HH6lBW74BHlj1l1ZlSW80C6cq71s90WW7xPqGg68wmH0W501gDB50_LdyW4GdL401gQkNbN7VryDj8_RkwW1stSKy3DnRHpW4Sn53q6fsK_gW2FcTSr6339cjW26QLpy8rG26MW2X7mbV3QYlrXW3TDgFf5C7JZMf4C-kJT04)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Treasuries as the base layer.** The cash-like end keeps broadening its footprint: **Franklin’s BENJI** [expanded to BNB Chain](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6sqv5nXHCW6N1X8z6lZ3kyW5jmvS83__RQtN2qpnpHV_3xQW7wH5xH2HpJzJN7xgkW2zRYLzN8X-vnRddBb4W8Q3JRw5bsVJdW7VBDLc6prcQSW7Qwtqh1qjS9xW7FpZKJ6s9Q1hW65pQc83Tdy5VW8STvjC8d7_8tW95ry1b19YDxzN7WjnhgHsmtdW5WVVt46VnX5QW6b6jPb3C437DW4nvGpc1wyQW9VnfDhK9kFBk-N3RBG-f8QdbLW1StK6H4Qw5nHW2mZBRv1FLjqGVMChm82nMWfXW7grxx-79848hW2RqjKZ7ms0k4W8R9yvD302CZQW8WNhV_8_WTwxW5SZtz58fYPqqW5B3GZp1tljHFW2RrP4J4sMXQvW5M5CNb4xF_K4W4dFN722MKsCsW9cMj554XTXp_Vn6_vm5H4QP-W8SxsN19kGXLmW8MzWkn7dXN6jW24Sxhb6KMbS2W4Mz3nk2y3LbKVsWShH6jCH_CW25r5_96y5yXkf5qvkHn04)
, while **Theo Network’s thBILL** (tokenized short-duration U.S. T-Bills) launched on **Ethereum** and **Arbitrum** and surpassed **$100M TVL**, with a starring role in **Arbitrum’s DRIP** (it became the #1 USD-eligible asset with $82M market size). Furthermore, **Galaxy** teased a **multichain tokenized money market fund** (Ethereum, Solana, Stellar) to unify institutional cash-equivalents. These expansions and new launches underscore a key point from the report: even as the opportunity set expands, **high-quality, liquid treasuries remain the credibility and liquidity anchor,** still highly appealing despite a growing menu of higher risk-return products.

![Chart-Oct-03-2025-03-04-43-3718-PM](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F195a65caa44041f74c1d7608ce4073ce68c61b80-1062x688.png&w=3840&q=75)

**Up the yield curve.** With that foundation solid, capital continues to move up the yield curve. **Grove** [deployed](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6srn3qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3mPW6gryrt47FsZkN7WzjYF9z6FmVGk9tP7bKZXwW1p3D0Y5CjkbMW78sGDb1rd_y6VvSvBR5gmf4PW7zmL9q4X9NmRVddtw224fLSRW7mDpsK64cFygW5Pnrtb1mZYPDW2vWX-R5Lgg0lW24dRV_6XZ4BHW8hbKbr84FRYCVWyvzt5-mW7gW76yJrC5TPXdpVbRPZy20F5xWW8xYljd671sFYW4LyPC324fgByW2LQ1-m3dqDptW6x2dZ11bZtPZW93y9318rtq_rW38XjR35s5-YVW2m0FYF6cpCg8N9kkRp31cRPnW4Tl1TM4nQFTyW5JqScr2h5X1DW5zFnQR952lrPW9k-GpV1p04JBf8GhjmK04)
 **$50M** into **Anemoy’s Tokenized Apollo Diversified Credit Fund (ACRDX)** on **Plume × Centrifuge**, bringing diversified credit onchain at launch scale. **Maple** [crossed](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6sr43qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3nBW5PrZ3J4yRDvFVJJP787T_HDjN2W2h5wVRWCwN7MwwbSgXq67N3mV-zQHfByyW85SZZV8RKq7rW67SgzZ5ZL3W7W2SvV9x42D0Y6W5F6KRb3WrXFNW1VLjln3g4SBgW1QN-8X3Zgj7WVdGgR_4tjsKvW7f2d4Q76hZKgW8XwXjJ93QLqGW8CGbww1bscgnW4pvys88JXYl9W5PgvBx59gCScW5yB9qC4587l0W8kwGkr3Rdmq8W6HlcQS30v7KMW1b0RKV6MVQZ3W8g8BbN13KZ5zW7vtRrd77DPlxW3ltrBG5DsDKKVl-pjF31xxlMVs9T0S1SkRwxf5b90q404)
 **$4B AUM:** **syrupUSDC, which also expanded to Arbitrum via** [DRIP](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6srn3qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3p1W941VQB5tN19_W9h0c7P1SFbLKW4sybBd8fTgZpW6Yz7Nh47BgHVW7nL4Bc5DHkpLVcDxgv3Y3_z_W5Bjd9R2jLP20VnyXVb6qln9KW2HRSBj7Lq4ZvW2zmZxQ1q0xwZN7BYYtHbMhYsW729XHg6vNkP4W43MxpP8TjqHJW8YxLvt604DnZW7vJRyk62wB7ZW7gjDsm6W0b3RW7bd7Zs7t-7tQW7RDSKK2Dpss2W1px1Bc5B2H_vW6DL_lm3Gq-79W8YHG007qcp-wN8LsYL3wSVTjW5S0Z_T4J3g9mMfjtkW44KDGW5-fhww8sK04nW5MZScP94s9tKW8nXzWJ5cpD79W5Y6KNN2mMtsTf6rnT2d04)
**,** accounts for **$2.6B** with ~**$1.2B** loans outstanding, while **syrupUSDT** ranks second with **$636M** (the **$200M** Plasma [vault](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6sr43qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3lMW5lKqC46XD0LwW3Nb_342HJyc3TldWb1FNN-HW2dc10m2W7CSfW6dpJ0Z1Kv8r8W5tzJnF2F7jK1W3fsRbt1jx5C1W5Dl-T_2zXdstW8yssW84cN0gmVjXmX-8JZdFQW5v0LX818yk_gW4PyHCB90vkDmW8bBg6k7hGY46N32jnHSBg-QPW6cg9sQ6fYHMLW1l5kT06yYpZcW1zZQzK302kPpW8W7B7C4FvN6ZW4Qn5FR6xrMvjW5wk9cG39Z3W5W770Bn56Gj2DKW3GcbwY8ljNjpW63WNw_4bYZgZMx7LJFQwk8SW7G4XzY4TSHLYW7dj4zF3fLKsMf97xw4R04)

The bridge from compliant assets to DeFi keeps strengthening: **Aave’s Horizon** RWA market is at **$180M** total size (**$134M** available, **$45M** borrows), with Superstate’s USCC ~$56M, RLUSD ~$54M, and Anemoy JTRSY ~$34M among leaders. **Centrifuge deRWA** [expanded](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6srn3qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3p4W6gFyHg6Cw9RgW9f4v1f41xQq5W8qxKkR1zGzsvW46xg2R24sYjzW6N7Rlq3jnpsNW6jFnW46d7nYBW48dgg063zr3-W5jrV_c4VD5bwW8wsz8Z6WSk75W4vLvFG7X3V9kW1MZVZD6zw4TtW6ZTW792v8zsTW8_4-711DxlmXW6sn-wv8z9cb9W8M-W7C53C191W7f6W8F4VNkP0W6gGb296d2pPmVtRL1d7SvB8kN15g-jKgg7xmW1DVSsb58WTBpW8KDfbR3vwV0VW6mBq2m2-mXz-W1K_NqV2fYGwpW5XWTKs47gttGW82qkgf6p2SPHW22MxQw8VfQfdN7LT9Vwmsd56W2ks0Y22y7-dsdVy69v04)
 to **Stellar** with **$20M** into deJTRSY and deJAAA, and **deJAAA** [crossed](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6sr43qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3l2W1HFZBv3z34KkW7Wghmj7tkzm7N4lM0fm-gv8qN3_kD6l-qklWW2TxpVx5WBYGCVRZsnr6yM9W5W8cMrbh28yXJ1W78fCY16NP49CW13Gl3_8hm_B3LmrjvB9NrLVWZMb-1N4cr1M-V8d-mb5KkW7GJcLQ4xqvX0W7S6lCd4sgGN7W7Rnlqf82ZWrKW4-0vRx45bb4HN7ZnHp_X9gk4W94PXry3fWlC_W6-Lxlg9fDyfJW60-pVK65g3kJW6P5_-34F65HSW1L8l8d2ZJfPLW4Vz18v8fM7VFW3-56TY8jlP5cW6hPQG135fXd3W3TM3r28FcCjXf4t8jJF04)
 **$1M+** DEX volume on **Base. Theo Network's thBILL tokenized Treasury fund** [integrated](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6srn3qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3nzMswk_tpH4ZcW1812G-6CFJDxW8Lc_ds1DvnDyW5yMWCp4GsWbJW3lNBjm8cQrddW70QY7v2vLMcDW4szTXj7PjknfVP9rlb3nXfj3W5FQ43977mzh8W6Vm6xb1hkWSXV4NfFv6xRZzkW6zMKHm2_BKk0W22qJjD7S4ZKtW2WrX9J7M-GJNW6Bfrtw45cgj-W7WkJtl9bFpmMN5Ky6lT84bj2N6thnX5l829RW4YwB1y1WkQcsW5SGtrB7NzML9W7t3sLd3-9w2TW3d5yhz9btzW-W7gsg4J4B_99nW5JlBg05KWk5dW5_pSxR44l1XfW2TJcwN7Xv-7tW7TCsv283sSw7W1KxZrh340Kvcf1k0SD604)
 **with Pendle in September 2025,** enabling users to trade future yields via Principal and Yield Tokens. All clear signs that RWAs are being recognized as valuable building blocks of the onchain economy.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Higher-yield products, **permissionless** formats, deeper **DeFi integration**, and **new asset categories** are pulling in a wider user base. On **Plume**, **Nest** keeps compounding user scale: **nTBILL** and **nCREDIT** are around **100K holders** each, and **nALPHA** is nearing **200K**; **nBASIS** just [went multichain](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6srn3qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3mTW4PLwrb5fFZj6MfnTlbW6FnJW1D_zsq65cYf4W1mK8hM8ct-SGW94-Dzw7TWB9tW3RDG8_5QqcddW5mkn-F6CYj5vW6XWb6j1nMSVcW8CQrG173mMvqVY14Vc5MyxWVVF5SS82ctbJfMLKRygbhzDxW5sMcMx2SlB3KW2WLCcR8lh3rnW8xnRCp6z91SMW8jfGBD8ptp0vW901pc230VcySW607kb15nM-XsW5vHvQ88jxkpdW85qBRl68GXgWW9hVSfN7BDn4tW6tcVm131nMMTW7-HRLY7t6pZ2W4fbwHC2RphmQW1z2J9C7nksH5W6WN6bM1kW4m_W7CmYdf4QWJKgW3544pJ3C2SXVf9fL7cv04)
 expanding to **Plasma**, and opening USDT flows into Nest. Beyond treasuries and credit, new categories continue to open the tent: **GAIB** [grew](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6srn3qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3pJV7Hxvx3yMPkFW8mHrYD6qJGmgW3Lhy105GzM6mW6cpTzw78VkNrW4gkRcX3K56QcW3s46MZ2HLyPMW8q3Nck7Bv0lsVYSNBK7k8JxfW2Zf3tB6fpZLgW4F6LYl9dzMmPN4-Zs1DMQfdxW6z5f-S14_Wm7W308QR56YL8BJW8xJDLN4L_wJHW6TCwgm7F9-QwN71NyXQk5hLPW4cg-f11gBCg0W80DV6f6Q2tp-W23KBFM5nKVKmW8vGrH982M5wvW9b_87F2GCYJDW2DWlBp9lBz8bW3Kh4hh8dnZSxW3NP6qP8TRpTYW3NsWGf2C0HSyV2F14q59kPJCW4Fn9PS72Q-QNW1ZhHWt18LZvbf1b9x1204)
 from **$72M → $115M TVL** (GPU-backed yield, plus a new **USD1 on BNB Chain** vault), and **Spiko’s EUTBL** [surpassed](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6sr43qn9qW7Y8-PT6lZ3kqW5q4yQQ1dqXvQN4btH5rF8dp3W52d6bQ2Fgx-QW5tdY7-25Nq4cW6PLnGV37D8pfW6QYG0p30-6wpVV6WDp4f9H_GW3W381G8kYqMkW2vhW0L4222_JW3PS8xS48XRDZW19zCy35RSndbW2ntYS489WqclW1XSYQz1N9J17W4y1D_h27ZzPrW7h6slH38ZhN0V1vzKw4Z3r4rN75LZ_vSN0q7W8bsxVm78NQ9yW9k7f6m3F8l1RW3X5tJW49MpwMVbM5hy2cvptYW2J5dPS1p3fvVW3Cfl_v6zh1ZSW5mxfCM56Zhc_VjWQl14csj8gW45LZ1S6Cg-6Kf27jW7q04)
 **1,500 holders** as non-usd money-market adoption widens.

**Infrastructure matters, too.**

 to **Sei** with **Apollo’s ACRED** as the first tokenized offering, while [XRP Ledger’s MPT](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6srn3qn9qW8wLKSR6lZ3m6W2Q3whm4RP2GSVr-V-g8Tt07rW8H-v0S66Cw_ZN8ptCQq4cXf0N7qhzBhmBPlPVh0jNb5hWdZhVSTL-l1-2RRLW3v76Rg4FjhWnW6JpJtw6p5bZZN1Fsj0lxPncyW1V1Bfr1JPT0bW5SFGgl11wRnhW4z-Ds540ZY6vW701fWN2BghMKW6nCSpT64dF2HVjLL386r3LxMW8v2Mbs4hsq3bW9hqDLw7L8_S3W5rbm8V26cCFMW5ZR2_h6bnbJjW2Xb7Nx7DCKmGW6lm2Gl3PHmL3N605PTTSJQKtW7Z-G7y41mmwLW6XPxQz5_lTDmW91K5WS2qkHkpVLsbN31rxpQkVTN3js4k3dltf1FNrN604)
 aims to streamline compliant RWA issuance and DeFi support.

![RWA report](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F924ebbb18950b717ebbaf49db0adfca9f0a0f9ca-1120x429.jpg&w=3840&q=75)

Bottom line: the flywheel is spinning—**quality base assets → higher-yield products → DeFi integration → broader access**.

If you haven’t yet, grab the full report for the data, dashboards, and theses behind the trendlines:

[Download the full report](https://djfc5c04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/RL+113/djFC5c04/VWzZfX2-yyFhW64_ZJZ7gFqWWV9TLwZ5DdM7sN1c6sqP3qn9qW7lCdLW6lZ3l_N8n-F1m77826W5q_0gh2qPz4WW7MX0264QLkmkW1r2Y6Z2vQPN4W54SWHY76HVRMN80S0_3KGzgjW1JD_fY4X0qF6W19cmXB7ZRS9yW9cnLcT5bcSd3W3xkg8p8JZk4xW5RMLz816d_bXW7GlL7Q3glcxhW4FZjM16qtPN7VWB7nT52R45wW3Hfm0v5qJXpTW5qq4R85pcZNrW8hJRwc3G5-LdW24gD_v47bkWfVN3r1N61HfV1W7RDWVf5VvBXqW4GrFxc7H669hW6Vm9m56y1p91W7ZPVCX8SGPxMW15qm-Z7PwH6wf3VfKZz04)

# Dune Product Announcement Mats Olsen - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

DuneProductAnnouncementMatsOlsen
================================

Dune's Co-Founder and CTO, Mats Olsen talks about the future roadmap of Dune and announces the Dune API! Dune's API allows you to make any query into an API endpoint in an instance. To learn more about the Dune API, visit https://dune.com/docs/api/. Mats' Twitter: https://twitter.com/mewwts
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Guide•December 6, 2022•1 min read

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

Dune's Co-Founder and CTO, Mats Olsen talks about the future roadmap of Dune and announces the Dune API! Dune's API allows you to make any query into an API endpoint in an instance. To learn more about the Dune API, visit [https://dune.com/docs/api/.](https://dune.com/docs/api/)
 Mats' Twitter: [https://twitter.com/mewwts](https://twitter.com/mewwts)

# Careers at Dune | Dune

Let's change the game!
======================

Join our mission to make crypto data accessible

Explore roles

We're a tight knit team of 60+ hard working people having an outsized impact on the crypto industry. We take pride in being ambitious and doing world class work while staying humble and lighthearted.

If you like being challenged and want to work together with a brilliant team on an important mission, you should join us on the roller coaster.

Our values
----------

Get to the heart of it

We communicate directly and concisely. We tackle difficult topics head on and believe that daring to have difficult conversations yields better outcomes, relationships and more trust.

Embrace the rollercoaster

Our ride requires a growth mindset and the ability to enjoy working on an endless stream of really hard problems. Discover opportunities to learn and grow where others see crises. A roller coaster ride is uncomfortable but will also make you smile and laugh if you embrace it.

Our integrity is priceless

Our integrity is revealed when it's costly to maintain. We have the courage to stand up for what is right even if we face adversity or if nobody is looking. Our integrity defines us and without it we have nothing.

One team, one dream

We are one team working together to realize our mission. Our priority is team over individual. We work as a tight knit group moving fast together across teams and functions.

Change the game

We are ambitious and care about having a massive impact on the world. We have the courage to make bold decisions and transform the world around us. We don't settle for small outcomes.

User empathy

We're here to make the lives of our users easier. We put ourselves in their shoes and obsess over solving their problems. We never let any of our efforts drift away from that pursuit.

Create magic

Take pride in your work and strive for excellence. We don't settle for uninspired efforts, but go the extra mile to make something magical.

Sense of urgency

We move fast and work hard to get things done. We have a bias for action because action produces information we can iterate on.

![Team photo 15](https://dune.com/assets/team-pics/67aa2ce740efd201d4e84d8a_Image-p-1080.webp?dpl=dpl_BSRJYSM9KHdvNzmZSqwZVa8xgNS1)

![Team photo 16](https://dune.com/assets/team-pics/67aa2d6361f5a26d65800289_Image-1-p-1080.webp?dpl=dpl_BSRJYSM9KHdvNzmZSqwZVa8xgNS1)

Benefits and perks
------------------

Salary in the top 25% of industry

Using market-leading data and calibrating the team to market yearly, we ensure we stay competitive (75th percentile).

Equity in the top 25% of industry

We want Duners to have a significant stake in Dune. Our scheme has great terms with a heavily discounted strike price & a 10-year exercise window.

Annual Stock Rewards for Outstanding Impact

To reward exceptional impact further, you can receive performance equity grants every year with the chance to double your initial grant over 4 years.

Pension + 401(k) Plan

Auto-enrollment into national pension schemes with monthly contributions.

Flexible Time Off

25 paid days off per year (excl. National Holidays) + flexible public holidays you can move to any day.

Distributed Team

We are a distributed team, across Europe and East Coast US with a remote-first approach.

Focus Time

Say goodbye to meeting overload! We believe in a healthy mix of async and sync work, so you can focus on what truly matters—no more wasted time on endless meetings!

Co-Working Travel Allowance

We know working in a remote setting can be isolating, so each Duner has a yearly travel allowance for connecting and co-working with teammates.

Global Offsites & Team Meetups

We get together every quarter in a different location as a company or team to connect, work together and have lots of fun.

Stipend for Home Office

An allowance for your at-home setup, to ensure you are happy, comfortable and productive. If you prefer a local co-working space, we'll pay for your desk.

Private Medical Insurance

Good health is important, so we offer private medical insurance, dental & vision as standard.

Parental, Foster and Adoptive Paid Leave

16 weeks leave for primary caregivers and 6 weeks for secondary caregivers at full pay, to use throughout the child's first year. Plus a 2-week part-time phased return.

### Open roles

### Learn more

# Sonic is Now Live on Dune - Blog | Dune

[All posts](https://dune.com/blog)

SonicisNowLiveonDune
====================

Bringing Transparency to a Fast-Growing Ecosystem
-------------------------------------------------

News•December 18, 2024•2 min read

[Sonicsonic](https://dune.com/blockchains/sonic)

dune-catalyst

data

![@Alsie L.](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F2d4297f3e37d59932719698a65a11cfb77fe3194-673x673.jpg&w=96&q=75)

[Alsie L.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alsieliu/)
Content Marketing Manager at Dune

![Sonic is Now Live on Dune](https://dune.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.sanity.io%2Fimages%2Ffox336rh%2Fproduction%2F42f720b1e7f4ae4091b56d45bba0be76abb739ba-1400x787.webp&w=3840&q=75)

We’re excited to announce that [Sonic](https://www.soniclabs.com/)
, an EVM-compatible Layer-1 blockchain, is now integrated with Dune. This integration provides developers and analysts with access to Sonic's on-chain data, enabling deeper insights into its performance and ecosystem.

> “
> 
> “Sonic’s cross-chain innovations and thriving NFT ecosystem are setting benchmarks in Web3. Dune now offers the data clarity needed to dive deep into its activity.” — Fredrik Haga, Co-Founder & CEO of Dune

Understanding Sonic
-------------------

Sonic is designed to offer exceptional scalability and speed, achieving over 10,000 transactions per second with sub-second finality. Its EVM compatibility ensures seamless integration with existing Ethereum-based applications, providing developers with a familiar environment to build and deploy dApps.

Sonic's ecosystem is rapidly expanding, supported by incentive programs such as the Innovator Fund and Fee Monetization, designed to attract developers and users to the platform. These initiatives aim to foster innovation and accelerate the adoption of apps within the Sonic ecosystem.

Exploring Sonic Data on Dune
----------------------------

With Sonic now accessible on Dune, users can:

*   **Analyze Network Activity:** Examine transaction volumes, smart contract interactions, and other key metrics to gain insights into network utilization and performance.  
    
*   **Monitor Ecosystem Growth:** Track the development and adoption of dApps within Sonic's ecosystem, including DeFi protocols and NFT platforms.  
    
*   **Assess Cross-Chain Interactions:** Investigate how Sonic facilitates interoperability with Ethereum and other blockchains, enhancing the versatility of decentralized applications.  
    

> “
> 
> “We’re really excited to see the Sonic network’s data on Dune, one of the most well-known data analytics tools in the industry.This is a meaningful step toward making blockchain data more accessible and useful for everyone.” — Michael Kong, CEO of Sonic Labs

What’s next?
------------

If you believe there's a blockchain that deserves to be part of our platform, we invite you to submit your proposals through our [Data Request list](https://dune.com/feedback/?b=6595c480143e3c7207d23019)
 here. Or, use [Dune Catalyst](https://dune.com/product/dune-catalyst)
 to integrate your blockchain and make it discoverable on Dune!

Resources:[‍](https://docs.dune.com/data-catalog/evm/viction/overview)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[How to create Dune Dashboards](https://docs.dune.com/home)
[Use Dune API‍](https://docs.dune.com/api-reference/overview/introduction)

The data must flow!
